etymology.story: 1640s, "on the other side of," from Latin ulterior "more distant, more remote, farther, on the farther side," comparative of *ulter "beyond" (see ultra-).

root.meaning: device that breaks an electrical circuit

morphology.mnemonic: in + device that breaks an electrical circuit

etymology.story: early 15c., "to pour in, introduce, soak (something in liquid)," from Latin infusus, past participle of infundere "to pour into, pour out; press in, crowd in; mix, mingle," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + fundere "pour, spread" (see found (v.2)).

late 14c., "state or fact of forgetting," from Old French oblivion (13c.) and directly from Latin oblivionem (nominative oblivio) "forgetfulness; a being forgotten," from oblivisci (past participle oblitus) "forget," originally "even out, smooth over, efface," from ob "over" (see ob-) + root of levis "smooth," from PIE *lei-w-, from root *(s)lei- "slime, slimy, sticky" (see slime (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 憔悴；消瘦下去（emaciate的过去分词）

etymology.story: 1660s, past participle adjective from emaciate..
late 14c., apparently formed in Middle English on model of length, breadth; from Old English deop "deep" (see deep) + -th (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 混合起来， 相混合；相交往

etymology.story: mid-15c., "to bring together," frequentative of Middle English myngen "to mix," from Old English mengan (related to second element in among), from Proto-Germanic *mangjan "to knead together" (source also of Old Saxon mengian, Old Norse menga, Old Frisian mendza, German mengen), from PIE *mag- "to knead, fashion, fit" (see macerate).

1590s, from Late Latin edibilis "eatable," from Latin edere "to eat," from PIE root *ed- "to eat" (source also of Sanskrit admi "I eat;" Greek edo "I eat;" Lithuanian edu "I eat;" Hittite edmi "I eat," adanna "food;" Old Irish ithim "I eat;" Gothic itan, Old Swedish and Old English etan, Old High German essan "to eat;" 
 Avestan ad- "to eat;" Armenian utem "I eat;" Old Church Slavonic jasti "to eat," Russian jest "to eat")..

meanings.definitionCn: 神秘的；不可理解的；不能预测的；不可思议的

etymology.story: "that cannot be discovered by searching, mysterious," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 大洪水前的；远古的；陈旧的；旧式的
大洪水以前的人；年迈的人；不合时宜的人

morphology.mnemonic: in front of, before; end + day日

etymology.story: "before Noah's flood," 1640s, formed from Latin ante- "before" (see ante) + diluvium "a flood" (see deluge (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 顽强的； 坚硬的； 不能弯曲的， 坚固的

etymology.story: 1590s of persons; 1650s, of substances; from un- (1) "not" + yielding (see yield (v.))..
1846, "insensible," from Greek anaisthetos "insensate, without feeling; senseless, stupid" (see anesthesia).

late 14c., from Old French reveler "reveal" (14c.), from Latin revelare "reveal, uncover, disclose," literally "unveil," from re- "opposite of" (see re-) + velare "to cover, veil," from velum "a veil" (see veil (n.)).

1795, "warehouse," from French dépôt "a deposit, place of deposit," from Old French depost "a deposit or pledge," from Latin depositum "a deposit," noun use of neuter past participle of deponere "lay aside" (see deposit (v.)).

root.meaning: hinder-hood; posterity in time, inferiority in rank;

morphology.mnemonic: hinder-hood; posterity in time, inferiority in rank; + ance

etymology.story: mid-15c., a hybrid from hindren (see hinder (v.)) on model of French-derived words in -ance..
1640s, plural of datum, from Latin datum "(thing) given," neuter past participle of dare "to give" (see date (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 理论的；理论上的；假设的；推理的

morphology.mnemonic: 神 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "contemplative," with -al (1) + Late Latin theoreticus "of or pertaining to theory," from Greek theoretikos "contemplative, speculative, pertaining to theory" (by Aristotle contrasted to praktikos), from theoretos "that may be seen or considered," from theorein "to consider, look at" (see theory).

meanings.definitionCn: 合法的；正当的；公正的；正直的

etymology.story: Old English rihtful; see right (adj.1) + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 听（诊）；测量，测…深；使发声；试探；宣告
声音，语音；噪音；海峡；吵闹；听力范围；[医] 探条
健全的，健康的；合理的；可靠的；有效彻底的

etymology.story: "free from special defect or injury," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 好客的， 殷勤的， 热情友好的； 宜人的； 易接受的， 开通的

etymology.story: "kind and cordial to strangers or guests," 1560s, from Middle French hospitable, which is formed as if from a Medieval Latin hospitabilis, from the stem of Latin hospitari "be a guest," from hospes (genitive hospitis) "guest" (see host (n.1)).

early 15c., impetous "rapid movement, rush;" 1640s, with modern spelling, "force with which a body moves, driving force," from Latin impetus "an attack, assault; rapid motion; an impulse; violence, vigor, force;" figuratively "ardor, passion," from impetere "to attack," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + petere "aim for, rush at" (see petition (n.))..
1560s; see dis- + courteous.

Old English byht "bend, angle, corner" (related to bow), from Proto-Germanic *buhtiz (source also of Middle Low German bucht, German Bucht, Dutch bocht, Danish bught "bight, bay"), from PIE root *bheug- (3) "to bend," with derivatives referring to bent, pliable, or curved objects (source also of Old English beag, Old High German boug "ring;" see bow (v.)).

1823, from French récalcitrant, literally "kicking back" (17c.-18c.), past participle of recalcitrare "to kick back; be inaccessible," from re- "back" (see re-) + Latin calcitrare "to kick," from calx (genitive calcis) "heel" (see calcaneus).

meanings.definitionCn: 讨厌的人；损害；麻烦事；讨厌的东西

etymology.story: 1630s (in an Oriental travel narrative), probably from Arabic darb "a beating," from daraba "he beat up" (see discussion in OED).
1610s, from Medieval Latin tyro, variant of Latin tiro (plural tirones) "young soldier, recruit, beginner," of unknown origin..

meanings.definitionCn: 裂变；分裂；分体；分裂生殖法

etymology.story: 1819, "division of a cell or organism," from Latin fissionem (nominative fissio) "a breaking up, cleaving," from past participle stem of findere "to split" (see fissure).
1570s, from Middle French rectangle (16c.) and directly from Late Latin rectangulum, from rect-, comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 放置 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French interposicion "interpolation, intercalation; suspension, break" (12c.), from Latin interpositionem (nominative interpositio) "an insertion," noun of action from past participle stem of interponere "to put between, place among; put forward," from inter- "between" (see inter-) + ponere "to put, place" (past participle positus; see position (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 易怒的；倔强的，难以对待的

etymology.story: "apt to quarrel," 1725, from fraction in an obsolete sense of "a brawling, discord" (c.
early 14c., from Old French ancesserie "ancestry, ancestors, forefathers," from ancestre (see ancestor); spelling modified in English by influence of ancestor..
mid-15c., from Middle French penalité and directly from Medieval Latin poenalitatem (nominative poenalitas), from Latin poenalis (see penal).

a Middle English merger of Old English line "cable, rope; series, row, row of letters; rule, direction," and Old French ligne "guideline, cord, string; lineage, descent;" both from Latin linea "linen thread, string, line," from phrase linea restis "linen cord," from fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 刻度;天平,磅秤;比例尺;规模;音阶;鱼鳞
植被；植物，草木；呆板单调的生活

morphology.mnemonic: 生长/植物 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1560s, "act of vegetating," from Middle French végétation and directly from Medieval Latin vegetationem (nominative vegetatio) "a quickening, action of growing," from vegetare "grow, quicken" (see vegetable).

1630s, "to make larger, increase," from French agrandiss-, present participle stem of agrandir "to augment" (16c.), ultimately from Latin ad "to" (see ad-) + grandire "to make great," from grandis (see grand (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵略主义；沙文主义；武力外交政策

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + watershed, separation between river valleys

root.meaning: to set or place in position

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/在…上 + to set or place in position

etymology.story: early 14c., "to rise above, go beyond," from Old French surmonter "rise above," from sur- "beyond"  (see sur- (1)) + monter "to go up" (see mount (v.)).
early 15c., "indicate, express, set forth," from the noun in English and from Anglo-French purporter (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 母权制；女家长制；女族长制；母系氏族

morphology.mnemonic: 母亲 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: formed in English 1881 from matriarch + -y (4)..

meanings.definitionCn: [医] 养生法；生活规则；政体；支配
迟钝的；圆头的；不锋利的

etymology.story: early 15c., "dull, blunted," from Middle French obtus (fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 放逐；排斥；按贝壳流放法放逐

etymology.story: 1640s, from Greek ostrakizein "to banish," literally "to banish by voting with potshards" (see ostracism).

meanings.definitionCn: 吵闹的，喧嚣的；难驾驭的

morphology.mnemonic: to share a secret with + ance

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French confidence or directly from Latin confidentia, from confidentem (nominative confidens) "firmly trusting, bold," present participle of confidere "to have full trust or reliance," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + fidere "to trust" (see faith).
1753, from eulogy + -ize.
"subtle, cunning, crafty," early 14c., from wile (n.) + -ly (1).

meanings.definitionCn: [数] 任意的；武断的；专制的

etymology.story: early 15c., "deciding by one's own discretion," from Old French arbitraire (14c.) or directly from Latin arbitrarius "depending on the will, uncertain," from arbiter (see arbiter).

meanings.definitionCn: 非常高兴的；欢跃的；狂喜的；欢欣鼓舞的

morphology.mnemonic: leap about, leap up, + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1650s, from Latin exultantem/exsultantem (nominative exultans/exsultans) "boastful, vainglorious," present participle of exultare/exsultare (see exult).

meanings.definitionCn: 漏洞；枪眼；换气孔；射弹孔

etymology.story: also loop-hole, mid-15c., from Middle English loupe "opening in a wall" for shooting through or admitting light (c.

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + rog + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: late 15c., a back-formation from interrogation or else from Latin interrogatus, past participle of interrogare "to ask, question." The Old French word was interroger (14c.) which yielded English interroge (late 15c.), now obsolete.
"trial, test of quality, test of character," mid-14c., from Anglo-French assai (see assay (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 机敏的；狡猾的，诡计多端的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin astutus "crafty, wary, shrewd; sagacious, expert," from astus "cunning, cleverness, adroitness," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps from Greek asty "town," a word borrowed into Latin and with an overtone of "city sophistication" (compare asteism).
"routine, monotonous, dull, commonplace," 1550s, probably a reduplication of hum.

meanings.definitionCn: (举止,态度等的)庄严,端庄;尊贵,高贵

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French dignite "dignity, privilege, honor," from Latin dignitatem (nominative dignitas) "worthiness," from dignus "worth (n.), worthy, proper, fitting" from PIE *dek-no-, from root *dek- "to take, accept" (see decent)..
1620s, from Latin emittere "send forth," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + mittere "to send" (see mission).
1660s, formed in English, with -cy + Latin caelibatus "state of being unmarried," from caelebs "unmarried," probably from PIE root *kaiwelo- "alone" + lib(h)s- "living.".

1590s, "boiling," from Latin ebullientem (nominative ebulliens), present participle of ebullire "to boil over," literally or figuratively, from ex- "out" (see ex-) + bullire "to bubble" (see boil (v.)).
1540s, "one consecrated by a vow," from Latin votum "a promise to a god; that which is promised" (see vow (n.)) + -ary.
late 14c., attested in Anglo-Latin from mid-13c., via Old French almanach or directly from Medieval Latin almanachus, which is of uncertain origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 数/数量 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin numerosus "numerous," from numerus "a number" (see number (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 装备；供以军用品，供以服装

etymology.story: also accoutre, 1590s, from French acoutrer, earlier acostrer (13c.) "arrange, dispose, put on (clothing)," originally "sew up," from Vulgar Latin accosturare "to sew together, sew up," from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + *consutura "a sewing together," from Latin consutus, past participle of consuere "to sew together," from con- (see com-) + suere "to sew" (see suture).

early 14c., "burden, vex, inconvenience," from Old French encombrer "to block up, hinder, thwart," from Late Latin incombrare, from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + combrus "barricade, obstacle," probably from Latin cumulus "heap" (see cumulus).

meanings.definitionCn: 远的,遥远的,疏远的,偏僻的,细微的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French remot or directly from Latin remotus "afar off, remote, distant in place," past participle of removere "move back or away" (see remove (v.)).
1530s (in Anglo-Latin from mid-13c.), via French and Italian, from Arabic hashishiyyin "hashish-users," plural of hashishiyy, from the source of hashish (q.v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 结合，配合；动词的词形变化

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + jug + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin conjugationem (nominative conjugatio) "a combining, connecting," noun of action from conjugare "to join together" (see conjugal).

meanings.definitionCn: 使困惑，使模糊；罩入雾中

morphology.mnemonic: 破裂 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: "artifice, trick," late 15c., from Middle French strattegeme, stratagème "trick, especially to outwit an enemy" (15c.), from Italian stratagemma, from Latin strategema "artifice, stratagem," from Greek strategema "the act of a general; military stratagem," from strategein "to be a general, command," from strategos "general" (see strategy).

meanings.definitionCn: （人或动物的）尸体；残骸；（除脏去头备食用的）畜体

etymology.story: late 13c., from Anglo-French carcois, from or influenced by Old French charcois (Modern French carcasse) "trunk of a body, chest, carcass," and Anglo-Latin carcosium "dead body," all of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 模范；完美之物；优秀之人

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French paragon "a model, pattern of excellence" (15c., Modern French parangon), from Italian paragone, originally "touchstone to test gold" (early 14c.), from paragonare "to test on a touchstone, compare," from Greek parakonan "to sharpen, whet," from para- "on the side" (see para- (1)) + akone "whetstone," from PIE root *ak- "sharp, pointed" (see acrid)..

morphology.mnemonic: to play with + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French collusion, from Latin collusionem (nominative collusio) "act of colluding," from colludere, from com- "together" (see com-) + ludere "to play," from ludus "game" (see ludicrous).
"transparent, translucent," 1610s, from Latin pellucidus "transparent," from pellucere "shine through," from per- "through" (see per) + lucere "to shine" (see light (n.)).
1630s, from Latin fluctuatus, past participle of fluctuare "to undulate" (see fluctuation).

meanings.definitionCn: 固执的，坚持的；持久稳固的

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1723, from persistence or from Latin persistentem (nominative persistens), present participle of persistere (see persist).
early 13c., "eternal" (adj.); "eternally" (adv.); "eternity" (n.); from ever + lasting.

late 14c., "to hold, occupy, reside in" (without regard to ownership), a back formation from possession and in part from Old French possesser "to have and hold, take, be in possession of" (mid-13c.), from Latin possess-, past participle stem of possidere "to have and hold, possess, be master of, own," from posse "to be able," from potis "able, powerful" (see potent) + esse "to be" (see be).

meanings.definitionCn: 中立的，中性的；中立国的；非彩色的
中立国；中立者；非彩色；齿轮的空档

etymology.story: mid-15c., "one who remains neutral," from Latin neutralis "of neuter gender," (see neutral (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 政变；妙计；出乎意料的行动；砰然的一击

etymology.story: 1580s, "resolution of anything complex into simple elements" (opposite of synthesis), from Medieval Latin analysis (15c.), from Greek analysis "a breaking up, a loosening, releasing," noun of action from analyein "unloose, release, set free; to loose a ship from its moorings," in Aristotle, "to analyze," from ana "up, throughout" (see ana-) + lysis "a loosening," from lyein "to unfasten" (see lose).

meanings.definitionCn: 表面上的,貌似真实的；显然的,明明白白的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French aparant "evident, obvious, visible," from Latin apparentem (nominative apparens) "visible, manifest," present participle of apparere (see appear).

meanings.definitionCn: 纠纷；意见不合；争吵；倾轧

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French dissension (12c.) and directly from Latin dissensionem (nominative dissensio) "disagreement, difference of opinion, discord, strife," noun of action from past participle stem of dissentire "disagree" (see dissent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 暴露,揭露;方向;陈列;遗弃;照射量

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 放置 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1670s, a back-formation from placation or else from Latin placatus "soothed, quiet, gentle, calm, peaceful," past participle of placare "to calm, appease, quiet, soothe, assuage," related to placere "to please" (see please).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + sume + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-13c., "seizure and occupation without right," also "taking upon oneself more than is warranted," from Old French presumcion (12c., Modern French présomption) and directly from Late Latin praesumptionem (nominative praesumptio) "confidence, audacity," in classical Latin, "a taking for granted, anticipation," noun of action from past participle stem of praesumere "to take beforehand," from prae "before" (see pre-) + sumere "to take" (see exempt (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 谦逊的；简陋的；（级别或地位）低下的；不大的
使谦恭；轻松打败（尤指强大的对手）；低声下气

etymology.story: late 14c., "render oneself humble" (intrans.), also "to bend, kneel or bow;" late 15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 日用品；商品；农/矿产品；有用之物

etymology.story: early 15c., "benefit, profit, welfare;" later "a convenient or useful product," from Middle French commodité "benefit, profit," from Latin commoditatem (nominative commoditas) "fitness, adaptation, convenience, advantage," from commodus "suitable, convenient" (see commode).

meanings.definitionCn: 空气的；通风的；幻想的；轻快的；空中的

morphology.mnemonic: 空气 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "of the air, made of air," from air (n.1) + -y (2).

late 14c., "to have a certain weight," from stressed form of Old French peser "to weigh, be heavy; weigh down, be a burden; worry, be a concern," from Vulgar Latin *pesare, from Latin pensare "to weigh carefully, weigh out, counter-balance," frequentative of pendere (past participle pensus) "to weigh" (see pendant).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 向下/移除/取消 + 悬挂/称量/支付 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1610s, "not dependent on something else," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + dependent.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + 说话 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Late Latin elocutionem (nominative elocutio) "voice production, a speaking out, utterance, manner of expression," in classical Latin especially "rhetorical utterance, oratorical expression," noun of action from past participle stem of eloqui "speak out" (see eloquence).

meanings.definitionCn: 彬彬有礼的，温文尔雅的；都市化的

etymology.story: 1530s, "of or relating to cities or towns," from Middle French urbain (14c.) and directly from Latin urbanus "belonging to a city," also "citified, elegant" (see urban).

meanings.definitionCn: 所谓的；声称的；被断言的
宣称（allege的过去式和过去分词）；断言

etymology.story: mid-15c., "quoted," past participle adjective from allege.

meanings.definitionCn: 极小的；用草写小字写的

etymology.story: 1705, "small (not capital) letter;" as an adjective, "small," from 1727 (in printing; general sense of "extremely small" by 1893), from French minuscule (17c.), from Latin minuscula, in minuscula littera "slightly smaller letter," fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 貌似可信的，花言巧语的；貌似真实的，貌似有理的

etymology.story: 1540s, "acceptable, agreeable," from Latin plausibilis "deserving applause, acceptable," from plaus-, past participle stem of plaudere "to applaud" (see plaudit).
"lenient, willing to overlook faults," often in a bad sense, "too lenient," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 世界的； 世俗的， 现世的； 平淡的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "of this world," from Old French mondain "of this world, worldly, earthly, secular;" also "pure, clean; noble, generous" (12c.), from Late Latin mundanus "belonging to the world" (as distinct from the Church), in classical Latin "a citizen of the world, cosmopolite," from mundus "universe, world," literally "clean, elegant"; used as a translation of Greek kosmos (see cosmos) in its Pythagorean sense of "the physical universe" (the original sense of the Greek word was "orderly arrangement").

1540s, from Middle French insidieux "insidious" (15c.) or directly from Latin insidiosus "deceitful, cunning, artful, treacherous," from insidiae (plural) "plot, snare, ambush," from insidere "sit on, occupy," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

late 14c., "soda ash," from Medieval Latin alkali, from Arabic al-qaliy "the ashes, burnt ashes" (of saltwort, a plant growing in alkaline soils), from qala "to roast in a pan." The modern chemistry sense is from 1813..
1650s, from Latin fortuitus "happening by chance, casual, accidental," from forte "by chance," ablative of fors "chance" (related to fortuna; see fortune).

meanings.definitionCn: 怠惰的， 懒惰的； 迟钝的
古文物研究的；古文物的
古文物研究者；[古] 古文物收藏家

etymology.story: "one who studies or is fond of antiquities," c.

mid-14c., from Old French fervent "fervent, ardent" (12c.), from Latin ferventem (nominative fervens) "boiling, hot, glowing," figuratively "violent, impetuous, furious," present participle of fervere "to boil, glow," from PIE root *bhreuə- "to boil, bubble" (see brew (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 欺骗的；欺诈的；谎言的；虚伪的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from deceit + -ful.
early 15c., from Latin adducere "lead to, bring to, bring along," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 试演； 试听， 试音
[农] 施肥；[胚] 受精；肥沃

morphology.mnemonic: 搬运/承担 + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1857, noun of action from fertilize, or else from French fertilisation..

"edible substance in a nut or the stone of a fruit," Old English cyrnel "seed, kernel, pip," from Proto-Germanic *kurnilo- (source also of Middle High German kornel "a grain," Middle Dutch cornel "coarse meal"), from the root of corn "seed, grain" (see corn (n.1)) + -el, diminutive suffix.

meanings.definitionCn: 吝啬的，小气的；有刺的；缺乏的

etymology.story: "niggardly, penurious, extremely tight-fisted," 1650s, of uncertain origin, perhaps a dialectal alteration of earlier stingy "biting, sharp, stinging" (1610s), from sting (v.).
1590s, from Latin excavatus, past participle of excavare "to hollow out," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + cavare "to hollow, hollow out," from cavus "cave" (see cave (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 微妙的；精细的；敏感的；狡猾的；稀薄的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + sav + 具有…特征/名词化

meanings.definitionCn: 成分；组件；[电子] 元件

etymology.story: 1640s, "constitutional element" (earlier "one of a group of persons," 1560s), from Latin componentem (nominative componens), present participle of componere "to put together" (see composite).

meanings.definitionCn: 公平的，公正的；不偏不倚的

morphology.mnemonic: in + to separate the hair + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "not partial, not favoring one over another," 1590s, from assimilated form of in- (1) "not, opposite of" + partial.

meanings.definitionCn: 会诊医师,顾问医生;顾问

etymology.story: 1690s, of persons going to oracles, from consult + -ant.

meanings.definitionCn: 明白的；无争论之余地的

etymology.story: 1550s, from Late Latin indisputabilis, from in- "not, opposite of, without" (see in- (1)) + disputabilis, from Latin disputare "to weigh, examine, discuss, argue, explain" (see dispute (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 伪装；假装；用作伪装的东西

etymology.story: 1620s, "one side of a multi-sided body," from French facette (12c., Old French facete), diminutive of face "face, appearance" (see face (n.)).
"fictitious narrative," 1560s, from Italian novella "short story," originally "new story," from Latin novella "new things" (source of Middle French novelle, French nouvelle), neuter plural or fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 突出， 显著； 卓越； 重要

etymology.story: 1590s, "projection," from obsolete French prominence (16c.), from Latin prominentia "a jutting out" (see prominent).

meanings.definitionCn: 自由主义的；慷慨的；不拘泥的；宽大的

morphology.mnemonic: 自由 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1560s, "pertaining to marriage," from Latin genialis "pleasant, festive," literally "pertaining to marriage rites," from genius "guardian spirit" (see genius), with here perhaps a special sense of "tutelary deity of a married couple." Originally used in English in the Latin literal sense; meaning "cheerful, friendly" first recorded 1746.

1520s, "an abstract; brief statement of the chief points of some writing," from Middle French épitomé (16c.), from Latin epitome "an abridgment," from Greek epitome "an abridgment, a cutting on the surface; brief summary," from epitemnein "cut short, abridge," from epi "into" (see epi-) + temnein "to cut" (see tome).

meanings.definitionCn: 顽固的，执拗的；冷酷无情的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "stubborn; hardened," from Latin obduratus "hardened," past participle of obdurare "be hard, hold out, persist, endure," from ob "against" (see ob-) + durare "harden, render hard," from durus "hard" (see endure).

morphology.mnemonic: 正/直 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1620s, from French orthodoxie and directly from Late Latin orthodoxia, from late Greek orthodoxia "right opinion," noun of quality from orthodoxos (see orthodox)..

morphology.mnemonic: 模仿/复制 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1530s, a back-formation from imitation or imitator, or else from Latin imitatus, past participle of imitari "to copy, portray."
1610s, from Latin dominatus, past participle of dominari "to rule, dominate, to govern," from dominus (see domain).

meanings.definitionCn: 感触不到的；难理解的；无形的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French exceder (14c.) "exceed, surpass, go too far," from Latin excedere "depart, go beyond, be in excess, surpass," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + cedere "go, yield" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 迟钝的，迟缓的；不活泼的；麻痹的；[生物] 蛰伏的

etymology.story: 1610s, "benumbed, without feeling or power," from Latin torpidus "benumbed, stupefied," from torpere "be numb or stiff" (see torpor).

meanings.definitionCn: 防止，避免 ；转移(目光、注意力等)
无情的；麻木的；硬结的；起老茧的
使冷酷无情；使硬结；使生茧
原告；[贸易] 索赔人；提出要求者

etymology.story: 1747, from claim (v.), on model of appellant, defendant, etc., or from French noun use of present participle of clamer..
1530s, "vacant piece of ground," from Latin area "level ground, open space," used of building sites, playgrounds, threshing floors, etc.; which is of uncertain origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 君主，最高统治者；君主政体，君主国

morphology.mnemonic: 单一 + 统治/首领 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "state ruled by monarchical government," mid-14c.; "rule by one person," late 14c.; from Old French monarchie "sovereignty, absolute power" (13c.), from Late Latin monarchia, from Greek monarkhia "absolute rule," literally "ruling of one," from monos "alone" (see mono-) + arkhein "to rule" (see archon)..

meanings.definitionCn: 恶毒的；恶意的；堕落的；有错误的；品性不端的；剧烈的

etymology.story: late 14c., "unwholesome, impure, of the nature of vice, wicked, corrupting, pernicious, harmful;" of a text, "erroneous, corrupt," from Anglo-French vicious, Old French vicios "wicked, cunning, underhand; defective, illegal" (Modern French vicieux), from Latin vitiosus (Medieval Latin vicious) "faulty, full of faults, defective, corrupt; wicked, depraved," from vitium "fault" (see vice (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 能见度，可见性；能见距离；明显性
易于管教的；易驾驭的；易处理的；驯良的

etymology.story: "manageable," early 15c., from Latin tractabilis "that may be touched or handled, workable, tangible, manageable," figuratively, "pliant," from tractare "to handle, manage" (see treat (v.)).
late 14c., from Latin determinatus, past participle of determinare "to enclose, bound, set limits to" (see determine)..

meanings.definitionCn: 庞大的，巨大的；凶暴的，极恶的

etymology.story: 1530s, "abnormal" (usually in a bad sense), from Latin enormis "out of rule, irregular, shapeless; extraordinary, very large," from assimilated form of ex- "out of" (see ex-) + norma "rule, norm" (see norm), with English -ous substituted for Latin -is.

meanings.definitionCn: 怠惰，懒惰；[脊椎] 树懒

etymology.story: late 12c., "indolence, sluggishness," formed from Middle English slou, slowe (see slow (adj.)) + abstract formative -th (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 手法，技巧；熟练，灵巧；诡计
金属线制的；金属丝般的；坚硬的；瘦长结实的；（噪音）尖细的

etymology.story: 1580s, "made of wire," from wire (n.) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 外部的；表面的；[药] 外用的；外国的；外面的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + tern + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French externe or directly from Latin externus "outside, outward" (from exterus; see exterior) + -al (1).
1520s, "pay for work or services," back-formation from remuneration or else from Latin remuneratus, past participle of remunerari (later remunerare) "repay, reward" (see remuneration).

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 呼吸 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "one who aspires," 1738, from French aspirant, from Latin aspirantem (nominative aspirans), present participle of aspirare (see aspire)..

1530s, from Anglo-French paramont, Old French paramont "above" (in place, order, degree), mid-14c., from Old French par "by," from Latin per "through, for, by" (see per (prep.)) + amont "up," from a mont "upward" (see amount (v.)).

late 14c., from Old French salutacion "greeting," from Latin salutationem (nominative salutatio) "a greeting, saluting," noun of action from past participle stem of salutare "to greet" (see salute (v.)).
early 15c., from Old French agilité (14c.), from Latin agilitatem (nominative agilitas) "mobility, nimbleness, quickness," from agilis, from agere "to move" (see act (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 对人友好的，开朗的；出发的，外出的；即将离职的；乐于助人的

etymology.story: 1630s, "that goes out," from out (adv.) + going.
1640s, from Medieval Latin deleterius, from Greek deleterios "noxious," from deleter "destroyer," from deleisthai "to hurt, injure."

meanings.definitionCn: 社交的；好交际的；友善的

etymology.story: 1550s, "enjoying the company of others," from Middle French sociable (16c.) and directly from Latin sociabilis "close, intimate, easily united," from sociare "to join, unite," from socius "companion, ally" (see social (adj.))..

morphology.mnemonic: semi + 共同/联合 + 引导 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1838, "material whose electrical conductivity is between that of a conductor and that of an insulator," from semi- + conductor.

1570s, from Latin obsoletus "grown old, worn-out," past participle of obsolescere "fall into disuse, be forgotten about, become tarnished," which probably is from ob "away" (see ob-) + an expanded form of solere "to be used to, be accustomed" (see insolent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 仪器；工具；乐器；手段；器械

etymology.story: late 13c., "musical instrument, mechanical apparatus for producing musical sounds," from Old French instrument, enstrument "means, device; musical instrument" (14c., earlier estrument, 13c.) and directly from Latin instrumentum "a tool, an implement; means, furtherance; apparatus, furniture; ornament, dress, embellishment; a commission, authorization; a document," from instruere "arrange, furnish" (see instruct (v.)).

"lightest volatile liquid obtained from distillation of petroleum," 1864 (alternative spelling gasolene is from 1865), from gas (n.) + -ol (probably here representing Latin oleum "oil") + chemical suffix -ine (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 荒凉的；冷淡的，不好客的；不适居留的

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French inhospitable (15c.), from Medieval Latin inhospitabilis (equivalent of Latin inhospitalis), from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + Medieval Latin hospitabilis (see hospitable)..

"organic compound produced in animal bodies to regulate activity and behavior," 1905, from Greek hormon "that which sets in motion," present participle of horman "impel, urge on," from horme "onset, impulse," from PIE *or-sma-, from root *er- (1) "to move, set in motion." Used by Hippocrates to denote a vital principle; modern scientific meaning coined by English physiologist Ernest Henry Starling (1866-1927).
1840, from pyro- "fire" + mania "madness, frenzy." Used in German in the 1830s.

late 14c., from Old French eloquence (12c.), from Latin eloquentia, from eloquentem (nominative eloquens) "eloquent," present participle of eloqui "speak out," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + loqui "to speak" (see locution).

root.meaning: street noted for clubs, bars, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/外部 + street noted for clubs, bars, etc.

etymology.story: 1570s, "to pass in running," from out + Middle English strip "move quickly," of unknown origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + 起源/种类

etymology.story: early 14c., "beget, procreate," from Old French engendrer (12c.) "give birth to, beget, bear; cause, bring about," from Latin ingenerare "to implant, engender, produce," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + generare "beget, create" (see generation).

meanings.definitionCn: 植物的，植物性的；生长的
暗示 （inkle的ing形式）；略知；低声说出
大胆的； 有冒险精神的； 鲁莽的； 厚颜无耻的

etymology.story: 1540s, "confident, intrepid," from Middle French audacieux, from audace "boldness," from Latin audacia "daring, boldness, courage," from audax "brave, bold, daring," but more often "bold" in a bad sense, "audacious, rash, foolhardy," from audere "to dare, be bold." Bad sense of "shameless" is attested from 1590s in English.

meanings.definitionCn: 履行,执行;表演,演出;完成(事业)
回顾的；怀旧的；可追溯的

morphology.mnemonic: retro + 看/观察 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1660s, from retrospect + -ive.
"knavish deeds," 1520s, from knave + -ery..

meanings.definitionCn: 有密切关系的；贴切的；恰当的

etymology.story: mid-14c., "having the same parents," same as german (adj.) but directly from Latin germanus instead of via French (compare urbane/urban).

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + volve + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1620s, "an opening of what was rolled up," from Latin evolutionem (nominative evolutio) "unrolling (of a book)," noun of action from past participle stem of evolvere "to unroll" (see evolve).

early 15c., from Old French lucratif "profitable" and directly from Latin lucrativus "gainful, profitable," from lucratus, past participle of lucrari "to gain," from lucrum "gain, profit" (see lucre).

meanings.definitionCn: 解除；偿清；生命的终止；寂灭

etymology.story: "discharge, clearing of accounts," 1530s, short for Medieval Latin phrase quietus est "he is quit" (see quit).

meanings.definitionCn: 牧师的；牧人的；田园生活的；乡村的

morphology.mnemonic: 和平 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "of or pertaining to shepherds," early 15c., from Old French pastoral (13c.), from Latin pastoralis "of herdsmen, of shepherds," from pastor (see pastor (n.)).

mid-14c., "deliverance from sin," from Old French redemcion (12c.) and directly from Latin redemptionem (nominative redemptio) "a buying back, releasing, ransoming" (also "bribery"), noun of action from past participle stem of redimere "to redeem, buy back," from red- "back" (see re-) + emere "to take, buy, gain, procure" (see exempt).

morphology.mnemonic: bring one's influence to bear + 热 + 没有…的/无…的 + 状态/性质

etymology.story: late 14c., "lamentation, grief," from Old French complainte (12c.) "complaint, lament," noun use of fem.
1884; from pediatric; see -ics..
early 15c., "to take notice of," from Latin animadvertere "to notice, to take cognizance of," also "to censure, blame, punish," literally "to turn the mind to" (see animadversion).

meanings.definitionCn: 分析者；精神分析医师；分解者

etymology.story: 1650s, "mathematician skilled in algebraic geometry," from French analyste "a person who analyzes," from analyser (see analysis).

late 14c., etik (in fever etik "hectic fever"), from Old French etique "consumptive," from Late Latin hecticus, from Greek hektikos "continuous, habitual," also used of slow, continued diseases or fevers.

mid-14c., from Latin copiosus "plentiful," from copia "an abundance, ample supply, profusion, plenty," from com- "with" (see com-) + ops (genitive opis) "power, wealth, resources," from PIE root *op- (1) "to work, produce in abundance," (see opus).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "a beginning, a starting," from Old French inception and directly from Latin inceptionem (nominative inceptio) "a beginning; an undertaking," noun of action from past participle stem of incipere "begin, take in hand," from in- "in, on" (see in- (2)) + cipere comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 终止/界限 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1540s, "drive away," from Latin exterminatus, past participle of exterminare "drive out, expel, put aside, drive beyond boundaries," also, in Late Latin "destroy," from phrase ex termine "beyond the boundary," from ex- "out of" (see ex-) + termine, ablative of termen "boundary, limit, end" (see terminus).
early or mid-17c., false singular from Middle English pease (plural pesen), which was both single and collective (as with wheat, corn) but the "s" sound was mistaken for the plural inflection.

meanings.definitionCn: [语] 象形文字；古代石壁画；统计图表

etymology.story: "picture or symbol representing an idea," 1851, from Latin pictus "painted," past participle of pingere "to paint" (see paint (v.)) + -graph "something written." First used in reference to American Indian writing.

meanings.definitionCn: 给…蒙上阴影； 使扫兴； 使黯然失色

etymology.story: Old English ofersceadwian "to cast a shadow over, obscure;" see over + shadow (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 不合理的；昧着良心的；肆无忌惮的；过度的

etymology.story: 1560s, "showing no regard for conscience," from un- (1) + now rare conscionable "conscientious."

meanings.definitionCn: 时代错误；不合潮流的人或物

morphology.mnemonic: ana + 时间 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1640s, "an error in computing time or finding dates," from Latin anachronismus, from Greek anakhronismos, from anakhronizein "refer to wrong time," from ana- "against" (see ana-) + khronos "time" (see chrono-).
early 15c., from Latin assiduatem "continual presence," noun of quality from past participle stem of assiduus (see assiduous)..

morphology.mnemonic: to make up one's mind + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Medieval Latin decisivus, from Latin decis-, past participle stem of decidere (see decide).

late 14c., "unaccented syllable or note," from Latin thesis "unaccented syllable in poetry," later (and more correctly) "stressed part of a metrical foot," from Greek thesis "a proposition," also "downbeat" (in music), originally "a setting down, a placing, an arranging; position, situation," from root of tithenai "to place, put, set," from PIE root *dhe- "to set, to put" (see factitious).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + to swear profanely + …者/…器

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French precurseur and directly from Latin praecursor "forerunner," agent noun from past participle stem of praecurrere, from prae "before" (see pre-) + currere "to run" (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: （影视剧中的）配角；刻有浮雕的宝石或贝壳；小品文

etymology.story: early 15c., kaadmaheu, camew, chamehieux and many other spellings (from early 13c.
early 15c., "return to consciousness; restore to health," from Middle French revivre (10c.), from Latin revivere "to live again," from re- "again" (see re-) + vivere "to live" (see vital).
1520s, "scent with perfumes," back-formation from fumigation.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 折叠/交织 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "give a detailed account of," 1530s, from Latin explicatus, past participle of explicare "unfold, unravel, explain" (see explicit).

meanings.definitionCn: 干旱的；不毛的，[农] 荒芜的

etymology.story: 1650s, "dry, parched," from French aride (15c.) or directly from Latin aridus "dry, arid, parched," from arere "to be dry," from PIE root *as- "to burn, glow" (see ash (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 周期，期间；时期；月经；课时；（语法学）句点，句号

etymology.story: early 15c., "course or extent of time," from Middle French periode (14c.) and directly from Medieval Latin periodus "recurring portion, cycle," from Latin periodus "a complete sentence," also "cycle of the Greek games," from Greek periodos "cycle, circuit, period of time," literally "a going around," from peri- "around" (see peri-) + hodos "a going, way, journey" (see cede).

meanings.definitionCn: 无固定形状的； 无组织的

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 形态 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: "shapeless," 1731, from Modern Latin amorphus, from Greek amorphos "without form, shapeless, deformed," from a- "without" (see a- (3)) + morphe "form" (see Morpheus).

late 14c., "to go on," also "to emanate from, result from," from Old French proceder (13c., Modern French procéder) and directly from Latin procedere (past participle processus) "go before, go forward, advance, make progress; come forward," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + cedere "to go" (see cede).

1680s, "to look into" (transitive), from Latin introspectus, past participle of introspicere "look at, look into; examine, observe attentively," from intro- "inward" (see intro-) + specere "to look at" (see scope (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: 之间/相互 + 多 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story:
1610s, "to alter or enlarge (a writing) by inserting new material," from Latin interpolatus, past participle of interpolare "alter, freshen up, polish;" of writing, "falsify," from inter- "among, between" (see inter-) + polare, which is related to polire "to smoothe, polish."

Sense evolved in Latin from "refurbish," to "alter appearance of," to "falsify (especially by adding new material)." Middle English had interpolen (early 15c.) in a similar sense.

meanings.definitionCn: 解散；解雇；开除；让离开；不予理会、不予考虑

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin dimissus, past participle of dimittere "send away, send different ways; break up, discharge; renounce, abandon," from dis- "apart, away" (see dis-) + mittere "send, let go" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 逼真，貌似真实；逼真的事物

etymology.story: "appearance of truth or reality, likelihood," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 智力,聪明;理解力;情报,消息,报导

etymology.story: late 14c., "the highest faculty of the mind, capacity for comprehending general truths;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 功利的；功利主义的；实利的

etymology.story: 1781, coined by Jeremy Bentham (1748-1832) from utility + -arian on the model of + unitarian, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 自动机；机器人；自动机器

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin automaton (Suetonius), from Greek automaton, neuter of automatos "self-acting," from autos "self" (see auto-) + matos "thinking, animated, willing," from PIE *mn-to-, from root *men- "to think" (see mind (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 使蓬乱；使头发凌乱；弄乱

etymology.story: originally an adjective, "bare-headed," late 14c., variant (with muted final -e) of dishevely, from Old French deschevele "bare-headed, with shaven head," past participle adjective from descheveler "to disarrange the hair," from des- "apart" (see dis-) + chevel "hair," from Latin capillus "hair" (see capillary).

meanings.definitionCn: 体积，容量；大多数，大部分；大块
使扩大，使形成大量；使显得重要
古文物研究者；古董商人；收集古文物者
绝望；令人绝望的人或事
斯多葛学派哲学家；禁欲主义者；恬淡寡欲的人，不以苦乐为意的人
坚忍的，苦修的；斯多葛派的；禁欲主义的

etymology.story: late 14c., "philosopher of the school founded by Zeno," from Latin stoicus, from Greek stoikos "pertaining to a member of or the teachings of the school founded by Zeno (c.

morphology.mnemonic: 分配/免除 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French despensacion (12c., Modern French dispensation), or directly from Latin dispensationem (nominative dispensatio) "management, charge," noun of action from past participle stem of dispensare "disburse, administer, distribute (by weight)" (see dispense).

meanings.definitionCn: 环行,周线,巡回;电路,线路

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 拉紧/约束

etymology.story: 1530s, from Latin restrictus, past participle of restringere (see restriction).
1789, from Latin avunculus "maternal uncle," diminutive of avus (see uncle) + -ar.

mid-15c., "now, present, of the moment, current," from Old French instant "near, imminent, immediate, at hand; urgent, assiduous" (14c.) and directly from Medieval Latin instantem (nominative instans), in classical Latin "present, pressing, urgent," literally "standing near," present participle of instare "to urge, to stand near, be present (to urge one's case)," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + stare "to stand," from PIE root *stā- "to stand" (see stet).

meanings.definitionCn: 流浪者；流浪儿；飘流物；无主物；信号旗

etymology.story: late 14c., "unclaimed property, flotsam, stray animal," from Anglo-French waif (13c., Old French guaif) "ownerless property, something lost;" as an adjective, "not claimed, outcast, abandoned," probably from a Scandinavian source akin to Old Norse veif "waving thing, flag," from Proto-Germanic *waif-, from PIE *weip- "to turn, vacillate, tremble ecstatically" (see vibrate).

meanings.definitionCn: 废弃；使…过时；使…具有古旧形式或风格
本国的；土著的；天然的；与生俱来的；天赋的

etymology.story: "to make laws," 1805, back-formation from legislation or legislator.
early 15c., from Old French prolixe (13c.) and directly from Latin prolixus "extended," literally "poured out," from pro- "forth" (see pro-) + base of liquere "to flow" (see liquid (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 荣誉；荣誉称号授予仪式；连谱号；称赞

etymology.story: 1620s, from French accolade (16c.), from Provençal acolada or Italian accollata, ultimately from noun use of a fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 必要的，不可避免的；紧急的；命令的，专横的；势在必行的；[语]祈使的
必要的事；命令；需要；规则；[语]祈使语气

morphology.mnemonic: the British Empire, + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., in grammar; later "something imperative" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 线的，线型的；直线的，线状的；长度的

morphology.mnemonic: one's occupation, branch of business + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from French linéaire, from Latin linearis "belonging to a line," from linea "string, line" (see line (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 强健的；健康的；粗野的；粗鲁的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Middle French robuste (14c.) and directly from Latin robustus "strong and hardy," literally "as strong as oak," originally "oaken," from robur, robus "hard timber, strength," also "a special kind of oak," named for its reddish heartwood, from Latin ruber "red" (related to robigo "rust"), from PIE *reudh- (see red (adj.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 支配的,统治的,占优势的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Middle French dominant (13c.), from Latin dominantem (nominative dominans), present participle of dominari (see domination).

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 强壮/价值

etymology.story: 1918, a back-formation from devaluation.

morphology.mnemonic: 电 + 使…化/造成 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1748, "state of being charged with electricity," noun of action from electrify..

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 共同/联合 + 站立/设置 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1640s, "not agreeing in substance or form;" 1650s, "self-contradictory," from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + consistent.

meanings.definitionCn: 潦草写成的东西；潦草的写法；杂文

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Medieval Latin scribillare, diminutive of Latin scribere "to write" (see script (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 公开指责,公然抨击;谴责

etymology.story: early 14c., "announce," from Old French denoncier (12c., Modern French dénoncer), from Latin denuntiare "to announce, proclaim; denounce, menace; command, order," from de- "down" + nuntiare "proclaim, announce," from nuntius "messenger" (see nuncio).

meanings.definitionCn: 厌倦（jade的过去分词）；变得疲倦；精疲力竭

etymology.story: "bored by continual indulgence," 1630s; past participle adjective from jade (v.).
1751, student slang, "trick, jest, hoax, imposition, deception," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 干部会议；核心会议；党团会议
召开干部会议；开核心会议
无效的，不起作用的；徒劳无益的
无用的人；无一技之长者

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 出/从 + 做/制造 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + effectual.

morphology.mnemonic: 微小 + musical instrument, + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1590s, from Late Latin entitatem (nominative entitas), from ens (genitive entis) "a thing," proposed by Caesar as present participle of esse "be" (see is), to render Greek philosophical term to on "that which is" (from neuter of present participle of einai "to be;" see essence).

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 拥有/保持

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin prohibitus, past participle of prohibere "to hold back, restrain" (see prohibition).

meanings.definitionCn: （表面的）粗糙；（气候等的）严酷；艰苦的条件；（性格）粗暴

etymology.story: "economical in use," 1590s, from Middle French frugal, from Latin frugalis, from undeclined adjective frugi "useful, proper, worthy, honest; temperate, economical," originally dative of frux (plural fruges) "fruit, produce," figuratively "value, result, success," related to fructus (see fruit), from PIE *bhrug- "agricultural produce," also "to enjoy." Sense evolved in Latin from "useful" to "profitable" to "economical."

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 折叠/交织 + 统治

etymology.story: 1824, from Italian replica "copy, repetition, reply," from replicare "to duplicate," from Latin replicare "to repeat" (see reply (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 喧闹的酒会或宴会；一饮而尽

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French carousser "drink, quaff, swill," from German gar aus "quite out," from gar austrinken; trink garaus "to drink up entirely." Frequently also as an adverb in early English usage (to drink carouse)..

meanings.definitionCn: 评审员；确定税款的人；顾问

root.meaning: to estimate the value of property for the purpose of taxing it

morphology.mnemonic: to estimate the value of property for the purpose of taxing it + …者/…器

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French assessor "assistant judge, assessor (in court)" (12c., Modern French assesseur) and directly from Latin assessor "an assistant, aid; an assistant judge," in Late Latin "one who assesses taxes," literally "a sitter-by," agent noun from past participle stem of assidere "to sit beside" (see assess)..

"to hold or exercise sovereign power," late 13c., from Old French regner "rule, reign" (12c.), from Latin regnare "have royal power, be king, rule, reign," from regnum "kingship, dominion, rule, realm," related to regere "to rule, to direct, keep straight, guide" (see regal).
1831, from alkali (q.v.) + -oid.

meanings.definitionCn: 奴隶的；奴性的；卑屈的；卑屈的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin servilis "of a slave" (as in Servile Wars, name given to the slave revolts in the late Roman Republic), also "slavish, servile," from servus "slave" (see serve (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 多顾虑的， 谨慎的； 一丝不苟的， 严谨的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Anglo-French scrupulus, Middle French scrupuleux, or directly from Latin scrupulosus, from scrupulus (see scruple).
early 14c., kidenere, a word of unknown origin, perhaps a compound of Old English cwið "womb" (see bowel) + ey "egg" (see egg (n.)) in reference to the shape of the organ.

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1520s (especially in reference to the "Aphorisms of Hippocrates"), from Middle French aphorisme (14c., aufforisme), from Late Latin aphorismus, from Greek aphorismos "definition, pithy sentence," from aphorizein "to mark off, divide," from apo- "from" (see apo-) + horizein "to bound" (see horizon).
early 15c., from Middle French détracter, from Latin detractus, past participle of detrahere "to take down, pull down, disparage" (see detraction).
"a rough, quarrelsome person," 1808, American English, originally "lawless backwoodsman," probably from row (n.2).
"metal container to hold burning coals," 1680s, from French brasier "pan of hot coals," from Old French brasier, from brese "embers" (see braise)..

meanings.definitionCn: 股份，股票；库存；血统；树干；家畜

etymology.story: in reference to conversation, literature, "recurring, commonplace" (as in stock phrase), 1738, figurative use from sense "kept in store for constant use" (1620s), from stock (n.2)..

morphology.mnemonic: veri + 使…化/造成 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1520s, from Medieval Latin *verificationem (nominative verificatio), noun of action from past participle stem of verificare (see verify).

meanings.definitionCn: 延缓，推迟；使暂停；使悬浮

etymology.story: late 14c., from bald + -ness..

late 14c., alteration (Englishing) of earlier distract (perhaps by association with other past participle forms in -ght, such as caught, bought, brought), mid-14c., past participle of distracten "derange the intellect of, drive mad" (see distract)..

morphology.mnemonic: 错误地/不当地 + 战争

etymology.story: "conduct oneself improperly," late 15c.; see mis- (1) + behave.

meanings.definitionCn: 大声叫的；喊叫的，喧嚷的

morphology.mnemonic: 呼叫/声音 + ifer + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin vociferari "to shout, yell, cry out," from vox (genitive vocis) "voice"  (see voice (n.)) + stem of ferre "to carry" (see infer).

meanings.definitionCn: 仪式的；例行的；礼节性的

etymology.story: 1590s, from quarrel (n.1) + -some (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 健谈的；缠绕的；易旋转的

etymology.story: early 15c., "liable to constant change," from Middle French voluble, from Latin volubilis "that turns around, rolling, flowing," figuratively (of speech) "fluent, rapid," from volvere "to turn around, roll" (see volvox).

1781, "to make small," from be- + little (v.); first recorded in writings of Thomas Jefferson (and probably coined by him), who was roundly execrated for it in England:Jefferson used it to characterize Buffon's view that American life was stunted by nature, which he was refuting.

meanings.definitionCn: 任意的；无差别的；不分皂白的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 不/分离 + 判定/控告 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "not carefully discriminating, done without making distinctions," 1640s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + discriminate (adj.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 受到影响的；做作的；假装的
影响；假装；使…感动（affect的过去式和过去分词）

etymology.story: past participle adjective from affect (v.2); 1530s in the now-obsolete sense "favorably disposed" (preserved in disaffected); meaning "artificially displayed" is recorded from 1580s..
early 15c., "dissolved, of loose structure," also "morally lax," from Latin resolutus, past participle of resolvere "untie, unfasten, loose, loosen" (see resolution).

meanings.definitionCn: 指责，非难；抨击；反驳；对…表示怀疑

etymology.story: "attack by argument," late 14c., from Old French impugner (14c.), from Latin impugnare "to fight against, assault, attack," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + pugnare "to fight" (see pugnacious).

meanings.definitionCn: 诱惑,引诱;吸引,使感兴趣
奉献的（副词votively）；献纳的；诚心祈求的

etymology.story: 1590s, "dedicated or given in fulfillment of a vow," from Middle French votif, from Latin votivus "of or pertaining to a vow, promised by a vow, conforming to one's wishes," from votum (see vow (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 回报， 回应； 报答

etymology.story: "to return, requite," 1610s, back-formation from reciprocation, or else from Latin reciprocatus, past participle of reciprocare "rise and fall, move back and forth; reverse the motion of," from reciprocus (see reciprocal).
1660s, from a- (1) + gape (v.)..

late 14c., "old," from Latin inveteratus "of long standing, chronic, old," past participle of inveterare "become old in," from in- "in, into" (see in- (2)) + verb from vetus (genitive veteris) "old" (see veteran).

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 太阳/独自

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin absolvere "set free, loosen, acquit," from ab- "from" (see ab-) + solvere "loosen" (see solve).

morphology.mnemonic: 跟随 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, from Medieval Latin seclusionem (nominative seclusio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin secludere (see seclude)..

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + name名 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin denominatus, past participle of denominare "to name" (see denomination).

meanings.definitionCn: 并发症；复杂；复杂化；混乱

morphology.mnemonic: to make more complex + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French complication, from Latin complicationem (nominative complicatio), noun of action from past participle stem of complicare "to fold together, fold up, roll up," from com- "together" (see com-) + plicare "to fold, weave" (see ply (v.1)).
1580s, a back formation from procrastination or else from Latin procrastinatus, past participle of procrastinare "to put off till tomorrow; defer, delay" (see procrastination).

meanings.definitionCn: 乙醚；[有化] 以太；苍天；天空醚

etymology.story: late 14c., "upper regions of space," from Old French ether (12c.) and directly from Latin aether "the upper pure, bright air; sky, firmament," from Greek aither "upper air; bright, purer air; the sky" (opposed to aer "the lower air"), from aithein "to burn, shine," from PIE *aidh- "to burn" (see edifice).

meanings.definitionCn: 布阵；列阵；整军备战；严阵以待

etymology.story: late 14c., "prepare for a fight," from Old French embataillier "to prepare for battle," from assimilated form of en- (see en- (1)) + bataille (see battle (n.)).
1630s, "condition of being concealed, unobserved existence," from latent + -cy.

1570s, from Middle French accoster "move up to," from Italian accostare or directly from Late Latin accostare "come up to the side," from Latin ad- "to" (see ad-) + costa "rib, side" (see coast (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 在进行中的；徒步的；准备中
赦免；无罪开释；履行；尽职；（债务等的）清偿

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + to reward, give reward + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "payment of debt or retribution;" see acquit + -al (2).

late 13c., "to follow with hostile intent," from Anglo-French pursuer and directly from Old French poursuir (Modern  French poursuivre), variant of porsivre "to chase, pursue, follow; continue, carry on," from Vulgar Latin *prosequare, from Latin prosequi "follow, accompany, attend; follow after, escort; follow up, pursue," from pro- "forward" (see pro-) + sequi "follow" (see sequel).

late 14c., "common, ordinary," from Latin vulgaris, volgaris "of or pertaining to the common people, common, vulgar, low, mean," from vulgus "the common people, multitude, crowd, throng," perhaps from a PIE root *wel- "to crowd, throng" (source also of Sanskrit vargah "division, group," Greek eilein "to press, throng," Middle Breton gwal'ch "abundance," Welsh gwala "sufficiency, enough") [not in Watkins].

1530s, "reversal of what is expected" (especially a fatal turning point in a drama), from Latin catastropha, from Greek katastrophe "an overturning; a sudden end," from katastrephein "to overturn, turn down, trample on; to come to an end," from kata "down" (see cata-) + strephein "turn" (see strophe).

meanings.definitionCn: 运送， 搬运， 转运；表达； 传播

etymology.story: early 15c., "buy back, ransom," from Middle French redemer "buy back," from Latin redimere (see redemption).

1620s, "quality of having reason;" 1650s, "fact of being agreeable to reason," from French rationalité, from Late Latin rationalitas "reasonableness, rationality" (also source of Spanish racionalidad, Italian razionalita), from Latin rationalis (see rational)..

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 形状/形态 + ity

etymology.story: early 15c., conformyte, from Middle French conformité (14c.), from Late Latin conformitatem (nominative conformitas), from conformis "similar in shape," from conformare (see conform).

meanings.definitionCn: 一夫一妻制；[动] 单配偶，[动] 单配性

morphology.mnemonic: 单一 + 婚姻 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1610s, from French monogamie, from Late Latin monogamia, from Greek monogamia, from monogamos "marrying only once," from monos "single, alone" (see mono-) + gamos "marriage" (see gamete)..

meanings.definitionCn: 歇斯底里的；异常兴奋的

morphology.mnemonic: hyster + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "characteristic of hysteria," from Latin hystericus "of the womb," from Greek hysterikos "of the womb, suffering in the womb," from hystera "womb" (see hysteria).
"lethargy, listlessness," c.

meanings.definitionCn: 暗指，转弯抹角地说到；略为提及，顺便提到

etymology.story: 1530s, "mock," from Middle French alluder or directly from Latin alludere "to play, sport, joke, jest," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + ludere "to play" (see ludicrous).

meanings.definitionCn: 保管人，监护人；公共建筑看守

etymology.story: 1781, from custody (Latin custodia) + -an.

morphology.mnemonic: eu + phem + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1650s, from Greek euphemismos "use of a favorable word in place of an inauspicious one," from euphemizein "speak with fair words, use words of good omen," from eu- "good, well" (see eu-) + pheme "speech, voice, utterance, a speaking," from phanai "speak" (see fame (n.)).
1580s, from Middle French choral or directly from Medieval Latin choralis "belonging to a chorus or choir," from Latin chorus (see chorus)..
1610s, from French consécutif (16c.), from Medieval Latin consecutivus, from Latin consecutus "following closely," past participle of consequi (see consequence).

meanings.definitionCn: 吸收； 吸收和同化的过程

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 相似/相同 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "act of assimilating," from Old French assimilacion, from Latin assimilationem (nominative assimilatio) "likeness, similarity," noun of action from past participle stem of assimilare (see assimilate).
Old English in the literal sense; c.

morphology.mnemonic: adj. 右边的，右侧的 + ity

etymology.story: 1520s, from Middle French dexterité (16c.), from Latin dexteritatem (nominative dexteritas) "readiness, skillfulness, prosperity," from dexter "skillful," also "right (hand)" (source of Old French destre, Spanish diestro, etc.), from PIE root *deks- "on the right hand," hence "south" to one facing east (source also of Sanskrit daksinah "on the right hand, southern, skillful;" Avestan dashina- "on the right hand;" Greek dexios "on the right hand," also "fortunate, clever;" Old Irish dess "on the right hand, southern;" Welsh deheu; Gaulish Dexsiva, name of a goddess of fortune; Gothic taihswa; Lithuanian desinas; Old Church Slavonic desnu, Russian desnoj).
"number of times one quantity is contained in another," mid-15c., from Latin quotiens "how often? how many times?; as often as," pronominal adverb of time, from quot "how many?" (see quote (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 放弃；脱离关系；拒绝承认；抛弃；弃权

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of renouncing," from Latin renuntiationem (nominative renuntiatio), noun of action from past participle stem of renuntiare "renounce" (see renounce)..
1650s, from drain + -age..

1715, from Latin cilia, plural of cilium "eyelid, eyelash," perhaps related to celare "to cover, hide," from PIE root *kel- "to conceal" (see cell), but words for this part of the face can be tricky (see brow).

meanings.definitionCn: 无法解释的；破译不出的；难辨认的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 向下/移除/取消 + to write in code + able

etymology.story: 1802, from in- (1) "not" + decipherable (see decipher (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 两/双 + 婚姻 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "state of having two wives or husbands at the same time," mid-13c., from Old French bigamie (13c.), from Church Latin bigamia, from Late Latin bigamus "twice married," a hybrid from bi- "double" (see bi-) + Greek gamos "marrying" (see gamete).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 移动/迁移 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "to pass into a place as a new inhabitant or resident," especially "to move to a country where one is not a native, for the purpose of settling permanently there," 1620s, from Latin immigratum, past participle of imigrare "to remove, go into, move in," from assimilated form of in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + migrare "to move" (see migration).

meanings.definitionCn: 器械，器具，仪器；机构，组织

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin apparatus "tools, implements, equipment; preparation, a preparing," noun of state from past participle stem of apparare "prepare," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + parare "make ready" (see pare)..

early 15c., "write before, prefix," from Latin proscribere "publish in writing" (literally "write in front of"), including senses of "publish as having forfeited one's property, condemn, outlaw before the world," from pro- "before" (see pro-) + scribere "to write" (see script (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 衰退，[核] 衰减；腐烂，腐朽
衰退，[核] 衰减；腐烂，腐朽
引导；感应；使…就职；征召入伍

etymology.story: late 14c., "introduce, initiate, especially into office or employment," from Latin inductus, past participle of inducere "to lead into, introduce" (see induce).

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: "small, ornamental human representation in pottery or other material work," 1854, from French figurine (16c.), from Italian figurina, diminutive of figura, from Latin figura "shape, form, figure" (see figure (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 自由 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "free choice, freedom to do as one chooses," from Old French liberté "freedom, liberty, free will" (14c.), from Latin libertatem (nominative libertas) "freedom, condition of a free man; absence of restraint; permission," from liber "free" (see liberal)Nautical sense of "leave of absence" is from 1758.
1840, from French camaraderie, from camarade "comrade" (see comrade)..

Old English mersc, merisc "marsh, swamp," from Proto-Germanic *marisko (source also of Old Frisian and Old Saxon marsk "marsh," Middle Dutch mersch, Dutch mars, German Marsch, Danish marsk), probably from Proto-Germanic *mari- "sea" (see mere (n.))..

morphology.mnemonic: 人类 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "science of the natural history of man," 1590s, originally especially of the relation between physiology and psychology, from Modern Latin anthropologia or coined independently in English from anthropo- + -logy.

late 14c., "gaping void," from Old French chaos (14c.) or directly from Latin chaos, from Greek khaos "abyss, that which gapes wide open, is vast and empty," from *khnwos, from PIE root *gheu- "to gape, yawn" (source also of Greek khaino "I yawn," Old English ginian, Old Norse ginnunga-gap; see yawn (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 吸收；全神贯注，专心致志

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + sorb + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1590s, from Latin absorptionem (nominative absorptio), noun of action from past participle stem of absorbere "swallow up" (see absorb)..
late 14c., from Old French abundant and directly from Latin abundantem (nominative abundans) "overflowing," present participle of abundare "to overflow" (see abound).
late 14c., from Latin pigmentum "coloring matter, pigment, paint," figuratively "ornament," from stem of pingere "to color, paint" (see paint (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 在前/预先 + that which is cast

etymology.story: 1640s (as a past participle adjective, 1570s), from Late Latin deterioratus, past participle of deteriorare "get worse, make worse," from Latin deterior "worse, lower, inferior, meaner," contrastive of *deter "bad, lower," from PIE *de-tero-, from demonstrative stem *de- (see de).

meanings.definitionCn: [自][电子] 输入；将…输入电脑

etymology.story: late 14c., "put on, impose," from in (adv.) + put (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 引诱,劝使;引起,导致;感应

etymology.story: formerly also enduce, late 14c., "to lead by persuasions or other influences," from Latin inducere "lead into, bring in, introduce, conduct; persuade; suppose, imagine," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).
"to cut in two," 1640s, from Modern Latin bisectus, from Latin bi- "two" (see bi-) + secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 受过严格训练的人；良种动物
良种的；受过严格训练的；优秀的

etymology.story: 1701, of persons, "thoroughly accomplished," from thorough + past tense of breed.

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 标准/规范 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1835, displaced older abnormous (1742) and rival anormal (1835) under influence of Latin abnormis "deviating from a rule," from ab- "off, away from" (see ab-) + norma "rule" (see norm).

Old English wanian "make or become smaller gradually, diminish, decline, fade," from Proto-Germanic *wanen (source also of Old Saxon wanon, Old Norse vana, Old Frisian wania, Middle Dutch waenen, Old High German wanon "to wane, to grow less"), from *wano- "lacking," from PIE *we-no-, from root *eue- "to leave, abandon, give out" (see vain).

meanings.definitionCn: 伏特（电压单位）；环骑；闪避

etymology.story: unit of electromotive force, 1873, back-formation from voltaic..

mid-14c., from Old French difference (12c.) "difference, distinction; argument, dispute," from Latin differentia "diversity, difference," from differentem (nominative differens), present participle of differre "to set apart" (see differ).

1610s, from Latin extricatus, past participle of extricare "disentangle," figuratively "clear up, unravel," perhaps from ex- "out of" + tricae (plural) "perplexities, hindrances," which is of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: [化学] 分子；微小颗粒，微粒

etymology.story: 1794, "extremely minute particle," from French molécule (1670s), from Modern Latin molecula, diminutive of Latin moles "mass, barrier" (see mole (3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 怀旧的，回忆往事的；耽于回想的

morphology.mnemonic: indulge in reminiscences + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1705, from Latin reminiscentem (nominative reminiscens), present participle of reminisci "call to mind, remember" (see reminiscence).

meanings.definitionCn: 屁股；烟头；笑柄；靶垛；粗大的一端
[安全] 保护；保卫；保护措施

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French diligent (14c.), from Latin diligentem "attentive, assiduous" (see diligence).

meanings.definitionCn: 复杂的；精致的；久经世故的；富有经验的
使变得世故；使迷惑；篡改（sophisticate的过去分词形式）

morphology.mnemonic: 智慧 + …主义者/…专家 + ic + 使…/具有…的
统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1570s, from Latin anomalia, from Greek anomalia "inequality," noun of quality from anomalos "uneven, irregular," from an-, privative prefix, "not" (see an- (1)) + homalos "even," from homos "same" (see same)..

meanings.definitionCn: 适于抓握的；善于领会的

etymology.story: 1771, from French préhensile "adapted for grasping" (Buffon), from Latin prehensus, past participle of prehendere "to grasp, seize, get hold of," from prae- "before" (see pre-) + -hendere, from PIE root *ghend- "to seize, take" (see get (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 详细审查；监视；细看；选票复查

etymology.story: early 15c., "a vote to choose someone to decide a question," from Late Latin scrutinium "a search, inquiry" (in Medieval Latin, "a mode of election by ballot"), from Latin scrutari "to examine, investigate, search," from PIE root *skreu- "to cut; cutting tool" (see shred (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 现实主义；实在论；现实主义的态度和行为

etymology.story: 1794, from real (adj.) + -ism; after French réalisme or German Realismus; from Late Latin realis "real." Opposed to idealism in philosophy, art, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: (语言、词汇等)古代的，已不通用的

etymology.story: 1810, from or by influence of French archaique (1776), ultimately from Greek arkhaikos "old-fashioned," from arkhaios "ancient," from arkhe "beginning" (see archon).

1760, "deep gorge," from French ravin "a gully" (1680s, from Old French raviner "to pillage, sweep down, cascade"), and from French ravine "violent rush of water, gully worn by a torrent," from Old French ravine "violent rush of water, waterfall; avalanche; robbery, rapine," both ultimately from Latin rapina "act of robbery, plundering" (see rapine); sense influenced by Latin rapidus "rapid." Middle English ravine meant "booty, plunder, robbery" from c.

meanings.definitionCn: 不可剥夺的， 不可分割的

etymology.story: "that cannot be given up," 1640s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + alienable (adj.).
"four-sided," 1640s, with -al (1) + Latin quadrilaterus, from quadri- "four" (see quadri-) + latus (genitive lateris) "side" (see oblate (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 断层，裂口；巨大分歧，差别

etymology.story: 1590s, "deep crack in the earth," from Latin chasma, from Greek khasma "yawning hollow, gulf," related to khaskein "to yawn," and thus to chaos.

morphology.mnemonic: archaeological expedition + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French digestion (13c.), from Latin digestionem (nominative digestio), noun of action from past participle stem of digerere (see digest (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 腐败的；腐烂的；令人厌恶的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin putridus, from putrere "to rot," from putris "rotten, crumbling," related to putere "to stink," from PIE root *pu- (2) "to rot, stink" (see pus).

meanings.definitionCn: 险恶的；气候严酷的；狂风暴雨的

etymology.story: 1660s, from French inclément (16c.) and directly from Latin inclementem (nominative inclemens) "harsh, unmerciful," from in- "not, opposite of, without" (see in- (1)) + clementem "mild, placid." "Limitation to weather is curious" [Weekley]..

meanings.definitionCn: 征募； 从军， 参军； 谋取

etymology.story: also inlist, 1690s (trans.), 1753 (intrans.), from en- (1) "make, put in" + list (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 最新的，最近的；最迟的，最后的

root.meaning: sufficiently supported by facts or authority, well-grounded

morphology.mnemonic: sufficiently supported by facts or authority, well-grounded + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from Medieval Latin validatus, past participle of validare "to make valid," from validus (see valid).
1710, from guile + -less.

early 15c., "to forbid, prohibit," back-formation from inhibition or else from Latin inhibitus, past participle of inhibere "to hold in, hold back, keep back." Psychological sense (1876) is from earlier, softened meaning of "restrain, check, hinder" (1530s).

late 14c., from Old French desclos "open, exposed, plain, explicit," past participle of desclore (Modern French déclore) "open, break open, unlock, reveal," from des- (see dis-) + clore "to close" (see close (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 无法形容的；不能以言语表达的；坏透了的
不同的；不相干的；全异的
顾客， 主顾， 客户

etymology.story: 1560s, "body of professed adherents," from French clientèle (16c.), from Latin clientela "relationship between dependent and patron, body of clients," from clientem (nominative cliens; see client).
1560s, "to bring a building to ruin," from Latin dilapidatus, past participle of dilapidare "to squander, waste," originally "to throw stones, scatter like stones;" see dilapidation.

meanings.definitionCn: 挑衅的；目中无人的，蔑视的；挑战的

etymology.story: 1837, from French défiant, present participle of défier (see defy).

meanings.definitionCn: 无力的；[劳经] 无能力的，无权的

etymology.story: early 15c., "lacking might or fortitude," from power (n.) + -less.

morphology.mnemonic: read carefully + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 绘制…的图表；在海图上标出；详细计划；记录；记述；跟踪（进展或发展

etymology.story: 1620s, from French évoquer or directly from Latin evocare "call out, rouse, summon" (see evocation).

root.meaning: to cover with something in front

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + to cover with something in front
统治 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1797, from French antagonisme or directly from late Greek antagonisma, noun of action from antagonizesthai "to struggle against" (see antagonist)..

meanings.definitionCn: 敌手；[解剖] 对抗肌；[生化] 拮抗物；反协同试剂

etymology.story: 1590s, from French antagoniste (16c.) or directly from Late Latin antagonista, from Greek antagonistes "competitor, opponent, rival," agent noun from antagonizesthai "to struggle against, oppose, be a rival," from anti- "against" (see anti-) + agonizesthai "to contend for a prize," from agon "contest" (see agony).

mid-15c., from Old French inciter, enciter "stir up, excite, instigate" (14c.), from Latin incitare "to put into rapid motion," figuratively "rouse, urge, encourage, stimulate," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + citare "move, excite" (see cite).

early 15c., "moderation; intermediate state or amount," from Middle French médiocrité and directly from Latin mediocritatem (nominative mediocritas) "a middle state, middling condition, medium," from mediocris (see mediocre).

meanings.definitionCn: 不可原谅的；没法辩解的；不可宽恕的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin inexcusabilis "without excuse; affording no excuse," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + excusabilis, from excusare "apologize, make an excuse for" (see excuse (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 巨大的，广大的；无边无际的；非常好的

etymology.story: "great beyond measure," early 15c., from Old French immense (mid-14c.), from Latin immensus "immeasurable, boundless," also used figuratively, from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + mensus "measured," past participle of metiri "to measure" (see measure (v.)).
1640s, "to make void, to annul," from Latin vacatus, past participle of vacare "be empty, be void" (see vain).

1570s, from Latin inhaerentem (nominative inhaerens), present participle of inhaerere "be closely connected with, be inherent," literally "adhere to, cling to," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + haerere "to stick" (see hesitation).

morphology.mnemonic: a favoring circumstance + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1590s, formed in English from advantage, or else from French avantageux (15c.), from avantage (see advantage).

meanings.definitionCn: 侵入， 侵占， 侵犯； 侵蚀， 蚕食

etymology.story: late 14c., "acquire, get," from Old French encrochier "seize, fasten on, hang on (to), cling (to); hang up, suspend," literally "to catch with a hook," from en- "in" (see en- (1)) + croc "hook," from Old Norse krokr "hook" (see crook (n.)).
"exact copy," 1690s, two words, from Latin fac simile "make similar," from fac imperative of facere "to make" (see factitious) + simile, neuter of similis "like, similar" (see similar).

meanings.definitionCn: 明确的;一定的;意志坚强的,立场坚定的

etymology.story: 1550s, from Latin definitus "defined, bounded, limited," past participle of definire (see define).

late 15c., with -y (2) + ware, from Old English wær "prudent, aware, alert, wary," from Proto-Germanic *waraz (source also of Old Norse varr "attentive," Gothic wars "cautious," Old Saxon giwar, Middle Dutch gheware, Old High German giwar, German gewahr "aware"), from PIE root *wer- (4) "to perceive, watch out for" (see ward (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 重量，重力；负担；砝码；重要性

morphology.mnemonic: bring one's influence to bear + 热

etymology.story: "to load with weight," 1747 (figuratively, of the mind, from 1640s), from weight (n.).

late 15c., "interior, inward, internal," from Middle French intrinsèque "inner" (14c.), from Medieval Latin intrinsecus "interior, internal," from Latin intrinsecus (adv.) "inwardly, on the inside," from intra "within" (see intra-) + secus "along, alongside," from PIE *sekw-os- "following," suffixed form of root *sekw- (1) "to follow" (see sequel).
Old English gyltig "offending, delinquent, criminal," from gylt (see guilt (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: n中值，中位数；三角形中线；梯形中位线

etymology.story: "a median part," 1540s, from Latin medianus (see median (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 接近， 邻近； 亲近

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French proximité "nearness" (14c.), from Latin proximitatem (nominative proximitas) "nearness, vicinity," from proximus "nearest, next; most direct; adjoining," figuratively "latest, most recent; next, following; most faithful," superlative of prope "near" (see propinquity)..

meanings.definitionCn: 海的， 海事的； 海运的

etymology.story: 1540s, "of or pertaining to the sea," from Middle French maritime (16c.) or directly from Latin maritimus "of the sea, near the sea," from mare (genitive maris) "sea" (see mere (n.)) + Latin ending -timus, originally a superlative suffix (compare intimus "inmost," ultimus "last"), here denoting "close association with." Maritimes "seacoast regions of a country" is from 1590s; specifically of the southeasternmost provinces of Canada by 1926..

morphology.mnemonic: to injure, hurt, harm + 具有…特征/名词化 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., "abusive," from Old French injurios "unjust; harmful" (14c., Modern French injurieux) and directly from Latin iniuriosus "unlawful, acting unjustly, wrongful, harmful," from iniuria "injustice, unlawful violence, insult" (see injury).

meanings.definitionCn: 诡辩；挑剔；说模棱两可的话

etymology.story: "equivocate, evade the point, turn from the point in question or the plain truth," 1650s, from quibble (n.).
late 14c., from Old French plurel "more than one" (12c., Modern French pluriel), from Latin pluralis "of or belonging to more than one," from plus (genitive pluris) "more" (see plus).

"a ludicrous trick" [Johnson], 1520s, of uncertain origin, perhaps related to obsolete verb prank "decorate, dress up" (mid-15c.), related to Middle Low German prank "display" (compare also Dutch pronken, German prunken "to make a show, to strut").
Old English bulluc "young bull," from Proto-Germanic *bulluka-, from the stem of bull (n.1).
1840, from German Ozon, coined in 1840 by German chemist Christian Friedrich Schönbein (1799-1868) from Greek ozon, neuter present participle of ozein "to smell" (see odor).
