morphology.mnemonic: 大气/气象 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: "science of the atmosphere, weather forecasting," 1610s, from French météorologie and directly from Greek meteorologia "treatise on celestial phenomena, discussion of high things," from meteoron, literally "thing high up" (see meteor), + -logia "treatment of" (see -logy)..

morphology.mnemonic: watershed, separation between river valleys + able

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French divisibile, from Late Latin divisibilis, from divis-, past participle stem of Latin dividere (see divide (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 依教规的；权威的；牧师的

morphology.mnemonic: canon + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., "according to ecclesiastical law," from Medieval Latin canonicalis, from Late Latin canonicus "according to rule," in Church Latin, "pertaining to the canon" (see canon (n.1)).

1550s, from Middle French nomade (16c.), from Latin Nomas (genitive Nomadis) "wandering groups in Arabia," from Greek nomas (genitive nomados, plural nomades) "roaming, roving, wandering" (to find pastures for flocks or herds), related to nomos "pasture, pasturage, grazing," literally "land allotted," from PIE root *nem- "to divide, distribute, allot" (see nemesis)..
1670s, from Latin abradere "to scrape off" (see abrasion).
late 14c., from Latin convenientem (nominative conveniens), present participle of convenire (see convene)..

"pertaining to spring," 1530s, from Late Latin vernalis "of the spring," from vernus "of spring," from Latin ver "the spring, spring-time," from PIE *wesr- "the spring" (source also of Old Norse var "spring," Greek ear, Armenian gar-un, Sanskrit vasantah, Persian bahar, Old Church Slavonic vesna "spring," Lithuanian vasara "summer")..

morphology.mnemonic: 人/民众 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., from post-classical Latin populosus "full of people, populous," from populus "people" (see people (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 缩短， 削减； 剥夺

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French courtault "made short," from court "short" (Old French cort, from Latin curtus; see curt) + -ault pejorative suffix of Germanic origin.
early 15c., from Medieval Latin exhumare "to unearth" (13c.), from Latin ex- "out of" (see ex-) + humare "bury," from humus "earth" (see chthonic).
1660s, "bond, link, means of communication," from Latin nexus "that which ties or binds together," past participle of nectere "to bind," from PIE root *ned- "to bind, tie" (see net (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: [地质] 海侵；犯罪；违反；逸出

morphology.mnemonic: 穿越/转变 + 步/走 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French transgression "transgression," particularly that relating to Adam and the Fall (12c.), from Late Latin transgressionem (nominative transgressio) "a transgression of the law," in classical Latin, "a going over, a going across," noun of action from transgressus, past participle of transgredi "step across, step over; climb over, pass, go beyond," from trans- "across" (see trans-) + gradi (past participle gressus) "to walk, go" (see grade (n.)).

mid-14c., from Old French abondance and directly from Latin abundantia "fullness, plenty," noun of state from abundantem (nominative abundans), present participle of abundare "to overflow" (see abound)..

meanings.definitionCn: 量子论；额；美国昆腾公司（世界领先的硬盘生产商）

etymology.story: 1610s, "one's share or portion," from Latin quantum (plural quanta) "as much as, so much as; how much? how far? how great an extent?" neuter singular of correlative pronominal adjective quantus "as much" (see quantity).

meanings.definitionCn: 无与伦比的；出类拔萃的；无比的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French suasion (14c.) and directly from Latin suasionem (nominative suasio) "a recommending, advocacy, support," noun of action from past participle stem of suadere "to urge, incite, promote, advise, persuade," literally "recommend as good" (related to suavis "sweet"), from PIE *swad- "sweet, pleasant" (see sweet (adj.)).
1530s, from Middle French compétiteur (16c.), or directly from Latin competitor "rival," agent noun from competere (see compete)..

meanings.definitionCn: 大小；量级；[地震] 震级；重要；光度
摇动(液体)；使焦虑不安；困扰；鼓动

etymology.story: 1580s, "to disturb," from Latin agitatus, past participle of agitare "to put in constant motion, drive onward, impel," frequentative of agere "to move, drive" (see act (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 朦胧的；星云的，星云状的

etymology.story: late 14c., "cloudy, misty," from Latin nebulosus "cloudy, misty, foggy, full of vapor," from nebula (see nebula).

meanings.definitionCn: 免疫的,有免疫力的;有受影响的;豁免的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "free, exempt" (from taxes, tithes, sin, etc.), from Latin immunis "exempt from public service, untaxed; unburdened, not tributary," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + munis "performing services" (compare municipal), from PIE *moi-n-es-, suffixed form of root *mei- (1) "to change" (see mutable).

morphology.mnemonic: equi + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., a term in astrology (from French équation, 14c.); general sense of "action of making equal" is from 1650s, from Latin aequationem (nominative aequatio) "an equal distribution, a sharing in common," noun of state from past participle stem of aequare (see equal (adj.)).
early 13c., "hind part of a ship; steering gear of a ship," probably from a Scandinavian source, such as Old Norse stjorn "a steering," related to or derived from styra "to guide" (see steer (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 旋转式机器；[动力] 转缸式发动机

etymology.story: 1731, from Medieval Latin rotarius "pertaining to wheels," from Latin rota "a wheel, a potter's wheel; wheel for torture," from PIE root *ret- "to run, to turn, to roll" (source also of Sanskrit rathah "car, chariot;" Avestan ratho; Lithuanian ratas "wheel," ritu "I roll;" Old High German rad, German Rad, Dutch rad, Old Frisian reth, Old Saxon rath, Old Irish roth, Welsh rhod "carriage wheel").
mid-14c., from Old French brandiss-, present participle stem of brandir "to flourish (a sword)" (12c.), from brant "blade of a sword, prow of a ship," of Frankish origin (see brand (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: [化学] 化合物；混合物；复合词
合成；混合；恶化，加重；和解，妥协

etymology.story: late 14c., originally compouned, past participle of compounen (see compound (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 意志，意愿 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1610s, from French volition (16c.), from Medieval Latin volitionem (nominative volitio) "will, volition," noun of action from Latin stem (as in volo "I wish") of velle "to wish," from PIE root *wel- (2) "to wish, will" (see will (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使怀疑； 使丧失信誉， 使丢脸

etymology.story: 1550s, from dis- "opposite of" + credit (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 獾；獾皮；（大写）獾州人（美国威斯康星州人的别称）；<动><澳>毛鼻袋熊
非常的;格外的;意外的;离奇的;临时的

morphology.mnemonic: extra + 顺序/等级 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin extraordinarius "out of the common order," from extra ordinem "out of order," especially the usual order, from extra "out" (see extra-) + ordinem, accusative of ordo "order" (see order (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 一岁家畜；满一岁的动物

etymology.story: "animal a year old or in its second year," mid-15c., from year + -ling.

meanings.definitionCn: 爱慕， 吸引； 吸引力； 具有吸引力的事

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from French attraction, from Latin attractionem (nominative attractio) "a drawing together," noun of action from past participle stem of attrahere (see attract).
late 15c., "of the nature of an agreement," from Late Latin conventionalis "pertaining to convention or agreement," from Latin conventionem (see convention).
1620s, "to intertwine" (as a past participle adjective, early 15c.), from Latin complicatus "folded together; confused, intricate," past participle of complicare (see complication).

meanings.definitionCn: 变温和，变宽厚；减弱；缓和

etymology.story: late 14c., "to melt, soften, dissolve," from re- + Latin lentus "slow, viscous, supple" (see lithe), perhaps on model of Old French rallentir.
1650s, from French simplifier "to make simpler" (15c.), from Medieval Latin simplificare "to simplify," from Latin simplex "simple" (see simplex) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 聚精会神的；勤勉的；勤苦工作的

etymology.story: 1530s, from Latin sedulus "attentive, painstaking, diligent, busy, zealous," probably from sedulo (adv.) "sincerely, diligently," from sedolo "without deception or guile," from se- "without, apart" (see secret) + dolo, ablative of dolus "deception, guile," cognate with Greek dolos "ruse, snare."
"believable," late 14c., from Latin credibilis "worthy to be believed," from credere (see credo).

morphology.mnemonic: in + 伸展/趋向 + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1520s, from intention + -al (1) or else from Medieval Latin intentionalis.

meanings.definitionCn: 无感觉的；无情的；无生命的

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1510s, "lacking or deprived of physical senses," from Late Latin insensatus "irrational, foolish," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + sensatus "gifted with sense" (see sensate).

meanings.definitionCn: 轰动；巨型炸弹；一鸣惊人者

root.meaning: to make smooth or to give shape on a block

morphology.mnemonic: to make smooth or to give shape on a block + er

etymology.story: also block-buster, big bomb (4,000 pounds or larger, according to some sources), 1942, from block (n.) in the "built-up city square" sense.

early 14c., soudur, from Old French soldure, soudeure, from souder, originally solder, "to consolidate, close, fasten together, join with solder" (13c.), from Latin solidare "to make solid," from solidus "solid" (see solid (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 可忽略不计的，微不足道的

etymology.story: "capable of being neglected," 1819, from negligence + -ible.

morphology.mnemonic: 时间 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "worldly, secular;" also "terrestrial, earthly; temporary, lasting only for a time," from Old French temporal "earthly," and directly from Latin temporalis "of time, denoting time; but for a time, temporary," from tempus (genitive temporis) "time, season, moment, proper time or season," from Proto-Italic *tempos- "stretch, measure," which according to de Vaan is from PIE *temp-os "stretched," from root *ten- "to stretch" (see tenet), the notion being "stretch of time."

plane figure with five angles and five sides, 1560s, from Middle French pentagone or directly from Late Latin pentagonum "pentagon," from Greek pentagonon, noun use of neuter of adjective pentagonos "five-angled," from pente "five" (see five) + gonia "angle" (see -gon).
also set-back, 1670s, "reversal, check to progress," from set (v.) + back (adv.).

morphology.mnemonic: br + er + 行为/结果/集合

etymology.story: mid-15c., "a broker's trade," from broker (n.) + -age.
mid-14c., "a tiler" (early 13c.
1830, from French hydroxide; see hydro- + oxide..

morphology.mnemonic: 第一/首要 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., from Late Latin primordialis "first of all, original," from Latin primordium "a beginning, the beginning, origin, commencement," from primus "first" (see prime (adj.)) + stem of ordiri "to begin" (see order (n.)).
"customary manner, habit," c.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 相似/相同 + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1620s, from dis- + similar; perhaps on analogy of French dissimilaire.

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + to grow long, lengthen, + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: "to make long or longer," 1530s, from Late Latin elongatus, past participle of elongare "to prolong, protract" (see elongation).

meanings.definitionCn: 使磨损；变得令人紧张、急躁

etymology.story: "wear off by rubbing," c.

Old English weldan (Mercian), wieldan, wealdan (West Saxon) "have power over, compel, tame, subdue" (class VII strong verb; past tense weold, past participle gewealden), merged with weak verb wyldan, both from Proto-Germanic *waldan "to rule" (source also of Old Saxon and Gothic waldan, Old Frisian walda "to govern, rule," Old Norse valda "to rule, wield, to cause," Old High German waltan, German walten "to rule, govern").

meanings.definitionCn: 灾难性的；损失惨重的；悲伤的

etymology.story: 1580s, "ill-starred," from French désastreux (16c.), from désastre (see disaster) or from Italian desastroso.

meanings.definitionCn: 冲动；[电子] 脉冲；刺激；神经冲动；推动力

etymology.story: early 15c., "an act of impelling, a thrust, push," from Latin impulsus "a push against, pressure, shock," figuratively "incitement, instigation," past participle of impellere "to strike against, push against" (see impel).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + animal of vast size + ance

etymology.story: late 15c., from Middle French remonstrance (15c., Modern French remontrance), from Medieval Latin remonstrantia, from present participle stem of remonstrare "point out, show," from re-, intensive prefix (see re-), + Latin monstrare "to show" (see monster)..

meanings.definitionCn: 突出的，显著的；杰出的；卓越的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "projecting, jutting out," from Latin prominentem (nominative prominens) "prominent," present participle of prominere "jut or stand out, be prominent, overhang," from pro- "before, forward" (see pro-) + -minere "project, jut out," which is related to mons "hill" (see mount (n.1)).
1620s, "pertaining to irony," from Late Latin ironicus, from Greek eironikos "dissembling, putting on a feigned ignorance," from eironeia (see irony).

early 15c., from Late Latin permeabilis "that can be passed through, passable," from Latin permeare "to pass through, go over," from per- "through" (see per) + meare "to pass," from PIE root *mei- (1) "to change" (see mutable).

late 14c., "schoolmaster, teacher," from Old French pedagoge "teacher of children" (14c.), from Latin paedagogus, from Greek paidagogos "slave who escorts boys to school and generally supervises them," later "a teacher," from pais (genitive paidos) "child" (see pedo-) + agogos "leader," from agein "to lead" (see act (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 胆怯的；懦弱的；优柔寡断的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Late Latin pusillanimis "having little courage" (used in Church Latin to translate Greek oligopsychos "small-souled"), from Latin pusillis "very weak, little" (diminutive of pullus "young animal;" see foal (n.)) + animus "spirit, courage" (see animus).

morphology.mnemonic: 基础 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., "primary, original, pertaining to a foundation," modeled on Late Latin fundamentalis "of the foundation," from Latin fundamentum "foundation" (see fundament).

morphology.mnemonic: globe-shaped glass vessel + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1670s, "spherical," from globe + -al (1).

1520s, from Latin promulgatus, past participle of promulgare "make publicly known, propose openly, publish," perhaps altered from provulgare, from pro- "forth" (see pro-) + vulgare "make public, publish." Or the second element might be from mulgere "to milk" (see milk (n.)), used metaphorically for "cause to emerge;" "a picturesque farmers' term used originally of squeezing the milk from the udder" [L.R.

meanings.definitionCn: 顾问；法律顾问；参事（等于counsellor）

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 走/让步

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French exces (14c.) "excess, extravagance, outrage," from Latin excessus "departure, a going beyond the bounds of reason or beyond the subject," from stem of excedere "to depart, go beyond" (see exceed).

root.meaning: degree of hardness and resiliency in steel

morphology.mnemonic: degree of hardness and resiliency in steel + at + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: mid-15c., "fact of being tempered, proper proportion;" 1530s, "character or nature of a substance," from Latin temperatura "a tempering, moderation," from temperatus, past participle of temperare "to mix in due proportion, modify, blend; restrain oneself" (see temper (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 透视画法,透视图;远景,前途;观点,看法

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 看/观察 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "science of optics," from Old French perspective and directly from Medieval Latin perspectiva ars "science of optics," from fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 报复， 反击； 复仇

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin retaliatus, past participle of retaliare "requite, retaliate" (see retaliation).
"expert in tactics," 1761, from tactic + -ian..

meanings.definitionCn: 同等;平等;相等;等式;等同性

morphology.mnemonic: equi + 与…有关的/形容词 + ity

etymology.story: late 14c., "evenness, smoothness, uniformity;" c.

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 警告/提醒 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., amonicioun "reminding, instruction," from Old French amonicion "admonition, exhortation," from Latin admonitionem (nominative admonitio), noun of action from past participle stem of admonere "to advise, warn" (see admonish).

meanings.definitionCn: 反应，感应；反动，复古；反作用

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 做/驱动 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "action in resistance or response to another action or power," 1610s, from re- "again, anew" + action (q.v.).
"lying across," early 15c.

1660s (Milton), from Latin iubilantem (nominative iubilans), present participle of iubilare "to let out whoops," in Christian writers, "to shout for joy," related to iubilum "wild shout," from Proto-Italic *iu, an exclamation of joy that probably was in Proto-Indo-European (cognates: Greek iu, an interjection of amazement, iuge "crying;" Middle High German ju, juch, an exclamation of joy; Dutch juichen, Old Norse yla, English yowl).
1620s, from French adhésion or directly from Latin adhaesionem (nominative adhaesio) "a sticking to," noun of action from past participle stem of adhaerare (see adherent (adj.))..
"unvarying tone in music or speaking," 1640s; see monotony.
"to be fretfully discontented," mid-15c., probably from re-, here likely an intensive prefix, + pine (v.) "yearn."

meanings.definitionCn: 虚荣心强的；非常自负的

root.meaning: conceited, elated with a high opinion of oneself

morphology.mnemonic: conceited, elated with a high opinion of oneself + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., from vainglory + -ous, or from Old French vain glorios "boastful, swaggering."

root.meaning: a nation's collective, organized sea power

morphology.mnemonic: circum + a nation's collective, organized sea power + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1630s, from Latin circumnavigatus, past participle of circumnavigare "to sail round," from circum "around" (see circum-) + navigare (see navigation).
1912, see narcissism + -istic.

meanings.definitionCn: 离弃，丢弃；遗弃，抛弃；放弃

etymology.story: "a letting loose, surrender to natural impulses," 1822, from a sense in French abandon (see abandon (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: 走/让步 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., cessacyoun "interruption, abdication," from Latin cessationem (nominative cessatio) "a delaying, ceasing, tarrying," noun of action from past participle stem of cessare "delay" (see cease (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 冒渎；亵渎圣物；悖理逆天的行为
赞颂， 赞美， 颂扬

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 忍受/容忍 + able

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin intolerabilis "that cannot bear; that cannot be borne," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + tolerabilis "that may be endured," from tolerare "to bear, endure" (see toleration).

meanings.definitionCn: 操作；经营；[外科] 手术；[数][计] 运算

morphology.mnemonic: 工作 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action, performance, work," also "the performance of some science or art," from Old French operacion "operation, working, proceedings," from Latin operationem (nominative operatio) "a working, operation," from past participle stem of operari "to work, labor" (in Late Latin "to have effect, be active, cause"), from opera "work, effort," related to opus (genitive operis) "a work" (see opus).

meanings.definitionCn: 逆的，相反的；敌对的；不利的；有害的

etymology.story: late 14c., "contrary, opposing," from Old French avers (13c., Modern French adverse) "antagonistic, unfriendly, contrary, foreign" (as in gent avers "infidel race"), from Latin adversus "turned against, turned toward, fronting, facing," figuratively "hostile, adverse, unfavorable," past participle of advertere, from ad- "to" (see ad-) + vertere "to turn" (see versus).

meanings.definitionCn: 优先购买；强制收购；抢先占有；取代

etymology.story: 1630s, "reason, rationale," from Latin ratio "reckoning, numbering, calculation; business affair, procedure," also "reason, reasoning, judgment, understanding," from rat-, past participle stem of reri "to reckon, calculate," also "think" (see reason (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 连接，接合；交叉点；接合点

morphology.mnemonic: 连接 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1711, "act of joining," from Latin iunctionem (nominative iunctio) "a joining, uniting," noun of action from past participle stem of iungere "to join together" (see jugular).

meanings.definitionCn: [化学] 电解，[化学] 电解作用；以电针除痣

etymology.story: 1834; the name was introduced by Faraday on the suggestion of the Rev.

mid-15c., "to surround by hostile stratagem," from Latin circumventus, past participle of circumvenire "to get around, be around, encircle, surround," in figurative sense "to oppress, assail, cheat," from circum "around" (see circum-) + venire "to come" (see venue).

morphology.mnemonic: dwell居住 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., "customary, belonging to one's inherent disposition," from Medieval Latin habitualis "pertaining to habit or dress," from Latin habitus "condition, appearance, dress" (see habit (n.))..

root.meaning: to bring to a desired condition

morphology.mnemonic: to bring to a desired condition + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., condicionel, from Old French condicionel (Modern French conditionnel), from Latin conditionalis, from condicionem (see condition (n.)).

early 15c., "a prompting" (a sense now obsolete), from Old French instinct (14c.) or directly from Latin instinctus "instigation, impulse, inspiration," noun use of past participle of instinguere "to incite, impel," from in- "into, in, on, upon" (see in- (2)) + stinguere "prick, goad," from PIE *steig- "to prick, stick, pierce" (see stick (v.)).

"connoisseur in eating and drinking," 1820, from French gourmet, altered (by influence of Middle French gourmant "glutton") from Old French groume, originally "wine-taster, wine merchant's servant" (in 13c.
late 14c., from Old French redoutable (12c.), from redouter "to dread," from re-, intensive prefix, + douter "be afraid of" (see doubt (v.))..
1560s, from niggard + -ly (1).

morphology.mnemonic: 起源/种类 + 过程/状态

etymology.story: Old English Genesis, first book of the Pentateuch, which tells among other things of the creation of the world, from Latin genesis "generation, nativity," in Late Latin taken as the title of first book of the Old Testament, from Greek genesis "origin, creation, generation," from gignesthai "to be born," related to genos "race, birth, descent" (see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: [法] 遗嘱认证；处以缓刑；加以监护；在遗嘱认证法庭证明…精神失常
遗嘱检验；经认证的遗嘱；遗嘱认证法院管辖范围内的一切事项

etymology.story: mid-14c., "in the wrong direction," from awk "back-handed" + adverbial suffix -weard (see -ward).

meanings.definitionCn: 粗野的人；乡下人；外地人

morphology.mnemonic: one's particular business or expertise + 与…有关的/形容词

mid-13c., "to end at, to border on," from Old French aboter "join end to end, touch upon" (13c.), from à "to" (see ad-) + bout "end" (see butt (n.3)).
alternative (chiefly U.S.) spelling of anaemia (q.v.).

morphology.mnemonic: eu + 声音 + 具有…性质的

etymology.story:
Old English seoþan "to boil," also figuratively, "be troubled in mind, brood" (class II strong verb; past tense seaþ, past participle soden), from Proto-Germanic *seuthan (source also of Old Norse sjoða, Old Frisian siatha, Dutch zieden, Old High German siodan, German sieden "to seethe"), from PIE root *seut- "to seethe, boil."

Driven out of its literal meaning by boil (v.); it survives largely in metaphoric extensions.

"act of misleading someone," early 15c.; as a form of mental derangement, 1550s, from Latin delusionem (nominative delusio) "a deceiving," noun of action from past participle stem of deludere (see delude).

meanings.definitionCn: 奔放；兴高采烈；沸腾；冒泡

meanings.definitionCn: 律师；（加拿大）出庭律师（等于arrister-at-law）；（英）（有资格出席高等法庭并辩护的）专门律师

etymology.story: 1540s, "a student of law who has been called to the bar," from bar (n.3) in the legal sense + -ster.

early 14c., condempner "to blame, censure," from Old French condamner "to condemn" (11c.), from Latin condemnare "to sentence, doom, blame, disapprove," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + damnare "to harm, damage" (see damn).
1650s, from French lexicographe "lexicographer," from Greek lexikographos, from lexikon "wordbook" (see lexicon) + -graphos "writer," from graphein "to write" (see -graphy)..

mid-14c., "chief residence of a lord," from Old French mansion "stay, permanent abode, house, habitation, home; mansion; state, situation" (13c.), from Latin mansionem (nominative mansio) "a staying, a remaining, night quarters, station," noun of action from past participle stem of manere "to stay, abide," from PIE *men- "to remain, wait for" (source also of Greek menein "to remain," Persian mandan "to remain").

morphology.mnemonic: 拿/消耗 + 具有…特征/名词化

1670s, in figurative sense, of family trees; 1801 in general sense of "a derivative;" 1814 in literal sense, in reference to plants.

meanings.definitionCn: (Akin)人名；(土、瑞典、尼日利)阿金；(匈)奥金；(英)埃金

morphology.mnemonic: in front of, before; end + dat

1718, "committee of cardinals in charge of Catholic missionary work," short for Congregatio de Propaganda Fide "congregation for propagating the faith," a committee of cardinals established 1622 by Gregory XV to supervise foreign missions.

"original pattern from which copies are made," 1540s [Barnhart] or c.

meanings.definitionCn: 兽性的，卑劣的；残忍的；野蛮的

morphology.mnemonic: 战争 + 与…有关的/形容词

1630s, "to smoke" (tobacco), from vapor + -ize.

mid-14c., "escape inclusion in; lie beyond the scope of," from Old French transcendre "transcend, surpass," and directly from Latin transcendere "climb over or beyond, surmount, overstep," from trans- "beyond" (see trans-) + scandere "to climb" (see scan (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 时间 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

mid-15c., cohercen, from Middle French cohercer, from Latin coercere "to control, restrain, shut up together," from com- "together" (see co-) + arcere "to enclose, confine, contain, ward off," from PIE *ark- "to hold, contain, guard" (see arcane).
early 15c., "overcharge, charge too much expense," from Old French surcharger "to overload, overburden, overcharge" (12c.), from sur- "over" (see sur- (1)) + chargier "to load" (see charge (v.)).

"pertaining to or relating to horses or horsemanship," 1650s, formed in English from Latin equester (genitive equestris) "of a horseman, knightly," from eques "horseman, knight," from equus "horse" (see equine).
late 14c., "exercise of the power of reason; act or process of thinking logically;" also "an instance of this;" verbal noun from reason (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 唐吉诃德式的；狂想家的；愚侠的

also instil, early 15c., "to introduce (liquid, feelings, etc.) little by little," from Latin instillare "put in by drops; to drop, trickle," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + stilla "a drop" (see distill).
1660s, "good-tasting," from palate + -able.
1898, loan-translation of German Photosynthese, from photo- "light" (see photo-) + synthese "synthesis" (see synthesis).
also politick, "to engage in political activity," 1917, a back-formation from politics.

1590s, "mystically absorbed," from Greek ekstatikos "unstable, inclined to depart from," from ekstasis (see ecstasy).

1590s, from Latin occludere (past participle occlusus) "shut up, close up," from ob "against, up" (see ob-) + claudere "to shut, close" (see close (v.)).

late 14c., "of or pertaining to birthdays," from Latin natalis "pertaining to birth or origin," from natus, past participle of nasci "to be born" (Old Latin gnasci; see genus)..

1540s, from Middle French amplitude or directly from Latin amplitudinem (nominative amplitudo) "wide extent, width," from amplus (see ample).
late 15c., from bounty + -ful.

1540s, from Latin abstractus or else from the adjective abstract.

1540s, from Latin recuperatus, past participle of recuperare "to get again," in Medieval Latin "revive, convalesce, recover" (see recuperation).

meanings.definitionCn: 化石；僵化的事物；顽固不化的人

1610s, "any thing dug up;" 1650s (adj.) "obtained by digging" (of coal, salt, etc.), from French fossile (16c.), from Latin fossilis "dug up," from fossus, past participle of fodere "to dig," from PIE root *bhedh- "to dig, pierce."

Restricted noun sense of "geological remains of a plant or animal" is from 1736 (the adjective in the sense "pertaining to fossils" is from 1660s); slang meaning "old person" first recorded 1859.

Old English discipul (fem.
1580s, back-formation from munificence, or else from Latin munificent-, stem of munificus "bountiful, liberal, generous" (see munificence)..

etymology.story: 1823, from French autocratique, from autocrate, from Greek autokrates (see autocrat).

late 14c., from Old French arbitre or directly from Latin arbiter "one who goes somewhere (as witness or judge)," in classical Latin used of spectators and eye-witnesses, in law, "he who hears and decides a case, a judge, umpire, mediator;" from ad- "to" (see ad-) + baetere "to come, go." The specific sense of "one chosen by two disputing parties to decide the matter" is from 1540s.

1670s, earlier slogorne (1510s), "battle cry," from Gaelic sluagh-ghairm "battle cry used by Scottish Highland or Irish clans," from sluagh "army, host, slew," from Celtic and Balto-Slavic *slough- "help, service." Second element is gairm "a cry" (see garrulous).

"not producing the desired effect," 1650s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + efficacious.

"act on each other, act reciprocally," 1805, from inter- + act (v.).

etymology.story: "to have charge and direction of," 1610s, from Church Latin superintendere "to oversee" (see superintendent).
"number of persons, things, etc., regarded as a unit," early 15c., from noun use of Latin adjective aggregatum, neuter of aggregatus (see aggregate (adj.))..

Old English forsacan "object to, oppose, refuse, deny; give up, renounce" (past tense forsoc, past participle forsacen), from for- "completely" + sacan "to struggle, dispute, wrangle; accuse, blame" (see sake).

1650s, from French complicité, from Old French complice "accomplice, comrade, companion" (14c.), from Late Latin complicem, accusative of complex "partner, confederate," from Latin complicare "to fold together" (see complicate; also compare accomplice)..

meanings.definitionCn: 区别,辨别;有差别地对待,歧视

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 判定/控告 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin discriminatus, past participle of discriminare "to divide, separate," from discrimen (genitive discriminis) "interval, distinction, difference," derived noun from discernere (see discern).

1917, noun, verb, and adjective, from French camoufler, Parisian slang, "to disguise," from Italian camuffare "to disguise," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps a contraction of capo muffare "to muffle the head." Probably altered by influence of French camouflet "puff of smoke," on the notion of "blow smoke in someone's face." The British navy in World War I called it dazzle-painting..

meanings.definitionCn: 对…负有责任；对…做出解释；说明……的原因；导致；（比例）占

etymology.story: 1540s, "an adjunct, accessory," from French annexe, from annexer (see annex (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 统治者，君主；有权势的人

morphology.mnemonic: 力量/能力 + …的人/处于…状态 + 使…/具有…的
警告/提醒 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: "pertaining to money," 1802, from Late Latin monetarius "pertaining to money," originally "of a mint," from Latin moneta "mint, coinage" (see money).

early 14c., from Old French quantite, cantite (12c., Modern French quantité) and directly from Latin quantitatem (nominative quantitas) "relative greatness or extent," coined as a loan-translation of Greek posotes (from posos "how great? how much?") from Latin quantus "of what size? how much? how great? what amount?," correlative pronominal adjective, related to qui "who" (see who).

morphology.mnemonic: to oppose, dispute + 结果/过程/状态 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "presentation of formal arguments," from Old French argumentacion (14c.), from Latin argumentationem (nominative argumentatio) "the bringing forth of a proof," noun of action from past participle stem of argumentari (see argue).
"substance neutralizing poisons," 1892, from anti- + toxin.

meanings.definitionCn: （马等的）后膝关节；（马等的）[动] 后膝关节病

etymology.story: late 14c., "to choke, suffocate, drown," of uncertain origin, possibly an alteration of Old French estouffer "to stifle, smother" (Modern French étouffer), itself of uncertain origin, perhaps from a Germanic source (compare Old High German stopfon "to plug up, stuff").
"drunken revelry," 1630s, from the name of the Roman festival held in honor of Bacchus, from neuter plural of Latin bacchanalis (see bacchanal).

meanings.definitionCn: 破坏； 拆除； 驳倒， 推翻

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French demoliss-, present participle stem of démolir "to destroy, tear down" (late 14c.), from Latin demoliri "tear down," from de- "down" (see de-) + moliri "build, construct," from moles (genitive molis) "massive structure" (see mole (n.3)).

meanings.definitionCn: 人工受精；[畜牧] 人工授精
圆的；完全的；大概的；肥胖的
在周围；迂回地；朝反方向；挨个
大使；特使，(派驻国际组织的)代表

etymology.story: late 14c., also embassador, from Middle French ambassadeur, from Old French embassator, via Provençal or Old Spanish from Latin ambactus "a servant, vassal," from Celtic amb(i)actos "a messenger, servant," from PIE *ambhi- "about" (see ambi-) + *ag- "drive, lead" (see act (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 使变形；使改观；美化；理想化

morphology.mnemonic: 穿越/转变 + to shape into

etymology.story: early 13c., from Old French transfigurer "change, transform" (12c.), and directly from Latin transfigurare "change the shape of," from trans- "across" (see trans-) + figurare "to form, fashion," from figura "to form, shape," from figura "a shape, form, figure" (see figure (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 假装虔诚的；假装圣洁的；假装诚实的

etymology.story: 1580s, from mis- (1) + interpret.

meanings.definitionCn: 使动摇；使不安定；使心神不宁

etymology.story: 1590s, "undo from a fixed position," from un- (2) "reverse, opposite of" + settle (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 不明确的,含糊的,暧昧的

etymology.story: "uncertain as to specifics," 1540s, from Middle French vague "empty, vacant; wild, uncultivated; wandering" (13c.), from Latin vagus "strolling, wandering, rambling," figuratively "vacillating, uncertain," of unknown origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 司法的；法院的；裁判上的

morphology.mnemonic: 判断/法律 + id + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin resplendentem (nominative resplendens) "brilliant, radiant," present participle of resplendere "to glitter, shine," from re-, intensive prefix, + splendere "to shine, be splendid" (see splendid).
1834, introduced by English physicist and chemist Michael Faraday (suggested by the Rev.

morphology.mnemonic: creatures of a day. + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1560s; see ephemera + -al (1).

1570s, from French bragard (16c.), with pejorative ending (see -ard) + Middle French braguer "to flaunt, brag," perhaps originally "to show off clothes, especially breeches," from brague "breeches" (see bracket).

meanings.definitionCn: 不受惩罚；无患；[法] 免罚

etymology.story: 1530s, from Middle French impunité (14c.) and directly from Latin impunitatem (nominative impunitas) "freedom from punishment, omission of punishment," also "rashness, inconsideration," from impunis "unpunished, without punishment," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + poena "punishment" (see penal)..

meanings.definitionCn: 缓刑； 试用； 试用期

morphology.mnemonic: 测试/证明 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "trial, experiment, test," from Old French probacion "proof, evidence" (14c., Modern French probation) and directly from Latin probationem (nominative probatio) "approval, assent; a proving, trial, inspection, examination," noun of action from past participle stem of probare "to test" (see prove).

"steep face of rock," 1630s, from Middle French précipice, from Latin praecipitium "a steep place," literally "a fall or leap," from praeceps (genitive praecipitis) "steep, headlong, headfirst," from prae "before, forth" (see pre-) + caput "head" (see head (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 大学毕业生,研究生v.大学毕业a.毕了业的

etymology.story: early 15c., "to confer a university degree upon," from Medieval Latin graduatus (see graduate (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 全体一致的；意见一致的；无异议的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin unanimus "of one mind, in union," from unus "one" (see one) + animus "mind" (see animus).

morphology.mnemonic: 热 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1756, "having to do with hot springs," from French thermal (Buffon), from Greek therme "heat, feverish heat," from PIE *gwher- "to heat, warm" (source also of Latin fornax "an oven, kiln," formus "warm," Old English wearm; see warm (adj.)).
"scold," 1739, of unknown origin; perhaps related to Danish dialectal rag "grudge."

meanings.definitionCn: 洗礼；洗澡（常用复数）；斋戒沐浴

etymology.story: "ritual washing," late 14c., from Latin ablutionem (nominative ablutio), noun of action from past participle stem of abluere "to wash off," from ab- "off" (see ab-) + luere "wash," related to lavere (see lave)..
1620s (adjective and noun), from Greek diagnostikos "able to distinguish," from diagnostos, verbal adjective from diagignoskein (see diagnosis).

early 13c., "hardship, suffering, pain, bodily affliction," from Old French grief "wrong, grievance, injustice, misfortune, calamity" (13c.), from grever "afflict, burden, oppress," from Latin gravare "make heavy; cause grief," from gravis "weighty" (see grave (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 驾驶，操纵；使通过；航行于

root.meaning: a nation's collective, organized sea power

morphology.mnemonic: a nation's collective, organized sea power + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1580s, a back-formation from navigation, or else from Latin navigatus, past participle of navigare.

meanings.definitionCn: 有罪的；该责备的；不周到的；应受处罚的

etymology.story: late 13c., coupable, from Old French coupable (12c., Modern French coupable), from Latin culpabilis "worthy of blame," from culpare "to blame," from culpa "crime, fault, blame, guilt, error." English (and for a time French) restored the first Latin -l- in later Middle Ages..

mid-14c., from Old French excellent "outstanding, excellent," from Latin excellentem (nominative excellens) "towering, prominent, distinguished, superior, surpassing," present participle of excellere "surpass, be superior; to rise, be eminent" (see excel).

meanings.definitionCn: 射线；光线；【鱼类】鳐形目(Rajiformes)鱼
暂停，中止；[金融] 延期偿付

etymology.story: 1875, originally a legal term for "authorization to a debtor to postpone payment," from neuter of Late Latin moratorius "tending to delay," from Latin morari "to delay," from mora "pause, delay," from PIE *mere- "to hinder, delay." The word didn't come out of italics until 1914.

Old English wisnian, weosnian "to wither, dry up, waste away," from Proto-Germanic *wisnon (source also of Old Norse visna "to wither," Old High German wesanen "to dry up, shrivel, wither;" German verwesen "to decay, rot"), from PIE root *wei- (2) "to wither."
"contradict, deny, dispute," c.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + to appoint a time + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: 1530s, "coming before the proper or usual time," from un- (1) "not" + timely (adj.).

root.meaning: large group of people with common ancestry.

morphology.mnemonic: large group of people with common ancestry. + 与…有关的/形容词 + ity

etymology.story: 1690s, "national quality," from national + -ity (in some usages perhaps from French nationalité.

meanings.definitionCn: 巧妙的；狡猾的；有技巧的；欺诈的

etymology.story: 1610s, "learned, well-versed in the (liberal) arts," also "characterized by technical skill," from art (n.) + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 长篇大论；高谈阔论；热烈的演说
向…滔滔不绝地演讲；大声训斥

etymology.story: 1650s, from French haranguer (15c.), from Middle French harangue (see harangue (n.)).

late 14c., "to undergo or suffer" (especially without breaking); also "to continue in existence," from Old French endurer (12c.) "make hard, harden; bear, tolerate; keep up, maintain," from Latin indurare "make hard," in Late Latin "harden (the heart) against," from in- (see in- (2)) + durare "to harden," from durus "hard," from PIE *dru-ro-, from root *deru- "be firm, solid, steadfast" (see true).

meanings.definitionCn: 抽筋的；急动的；不平稳的

etymology.story: "characterized by jerks, spasmodic," 1819, originally in medical writing with reference to the pulse, from jerk (n.1) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 修辞的；修辞学的；夸张的

morphology.mnemonic: that which is spoken, + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-15c., "eloquent," from Latin rhetoricus, from Greek rhetorikos "oratorical, rhetorical; skilled in speaking," from rhetor "orator" (see rhetoric).

meanings.definitionCn: 初级读本；识字课本；原始物

etymology.story: late 14c., essencia (respelled late 15c.
1640s, partly formed in English from ornament (n.) + -al (1); partly from Latin ornamentalis, from ornamentum..

meanings.definitionCn: 杰出的才能， 高超的技艺， 专长， 造诣

etymology.story: late 13c., prouesse, from Old French proece "prowess, courage, brave deed" (Modern French prouesse), from prou, later variant of prud "brave, valiant," from Vulgar Latin *prodem (source also of Spanish proeza, Italian prodezza; see proud).

meanings.definitionCn: 伤感的；多愁善感的；感情用事的；寓有情感的

morphology.mnemonic: 感觉/感知 + 结果/过程/状态 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1749, "pertaining to or characterized by sentiment," from sentiment + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 球根的；球根状的；由球根生长的（等于bulbaceous）

etymology.story: 1570s, "pertaining to a bulb," from Latin bulbosus, from bulbus (see bulb).
1630s, from in- (1) "not, opposite of" + discernible.

meanings.definitionCn: 无所不能的；全能的；有无限权力的

morphology.mnemonic: 全部 + 力量/能力 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French omnipotent "almighty, all-powerful" (11c.) or directly from Latin omnipotentem (nominative omnipotens) "all-powerful, almighty," from omnis "all" (see omni-) + potens (genitive potentis) "powerful" (see potent).

meanings.definitionCn: 对…感到不满； 嫉妒； 勉强做， 不乐意地做

etymology.story: mid-14c., from be- + Middle English grucchen "to murmur" (see grudge).
1530s, "list of words with explanations," from Medieval Latin vocabularium "a list of words," from Latin vocabulum "word, name, noun," from vocare "to name, call" (see voice (n.)).
early 14c., from Anglo-French tretiz (mid-13c.), contracted from Old French traitis "treatise, account," from traitier "deal with; set forth in speech or writing" (see treat (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 多毛的；[昆] 有粗毛的

etymology.story: "hairy," 1620s, from Latin hirsutus "rough, shaggy, bristly," figuratively "rude, unpolished," related to hirtus "shaggy," and possibly to horrere "to bristle with fear" (see horror)..

mid-15c., from Anglo-French cupidite, Middle French cupidité, from Latin cupiditatem (nominative cupiditas) "passionate desire, lust; ambition," from cupidus "eager, passionate," from cupere "to desire" (perhaps cognate with Sanskrit kupyati "bubbles up, becomes agitated," Old Church Slavonic kypeti "to boil," Lithuanian kupeti "to boil over").

meanings.definitionCn: 抹去，抹掉；使自己不受人注意

root.meaning: to cover with something in front

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + to cover with something in front

etymology.story: "to erase or obliterate," especially something written or carved, late 15c., from Middle French effacer, from Old French esfacier (12c.) "to wipe out, destroy," literally "to remove the face," from es- "out" (see ex-) + face "appearance," from Latin facies "face" (see face (n.)).
1839, irregular formation from vegetable (n.) + -arian, as in agrarian, etc.

meanings.definitionCn: 侵略的，好斗的，有进取心的，敢作敢为的

etymology.story: 1791, from Latin aggress-, past participle stem of aggredi "to approach, attack" (see aggression) + -ive.

meanings.definitionCn: 弹性， 弹力； 复原力； 适应性； 乐观的性情

etymology.story: 1620s, "act of rebounding," from Latin resiliens, present participle of resilire "to rebound, recoil," from re- "back" (see re-) + salire "to jump, leap" (see salient (adj.)).

early 15c., "having one's belt fastened tightly," from Middle French succincte, from Latin succinctus "prepared, ready; contracted, short," past participle of succingere "tuck up (clothes for action), gird from below," from assimilated form of sub "up from under" (see sub-) + cingere "to gird" (see cinch (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 调和/和解 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French reconciliacion (14c.) and directly from Latin reconciliationem (nominative reconciliatio) "a re-establishing, a reconciling," noun of action from past participle stem of reconciliare (see reconcile)..
mid-14c., desdegne "scorn, contempt," earlier dedeyne "offended dignity" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: （组织的）层；[地质] 地层；社会阶层

etymology.story: "horizontal layer," 1590s, from Modern Latin special use of Latin stratum "thing spread out, coverlet, beadspread, horse-blanket; pavement," noun uses of neuter of stratus "prostrate, prone," past participle of sternere "to spread out, lay down, stretch out," from PIE *stre-to- "to stretch, extend," from root *stere- "to spread, extend, stretch out" (see structure (n.))..

early 14c., "open to view," from Old French overt (Modern French ouvert), past participle of ovrir "to open," from Latin aperire "to open, uncover," from PIE compound *ap-wer-yo- from *ap- "off, away" (see abo-) + base *wer- (5) "to cover" (see weir).

mid-15c., from Old French invasion "invasion, attack, assaut" (12c.), from Late Latin invasionem (nominative invasio) "an attack, invasion," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin invadere "to go, come, or get into; enter violently, penetrate into as an enemy, assail, assault, make an attack on," from in- "in" (see in- (2)) + vadere "go, walk" (see vamoose)..

early 15c., "case which may be taken as a rule in similar cases," from Middle French precedent, noun use of an adjective, from Latin praecedentum (nominative praecedens), present participle of praecedere "go before" (see precede).

late 13c., from Old French polie, pulie "pulley, windlass" (12c.) and directly from Medieval Latin poliva, puliva, probably from Medieval Greek *polidia, plural of *polidion "little pivot," diminutive of Greek polos "pivot, axis" (see pole (n.2)).

meanings.definitionCn: 和谐的；协调的；一致的；和声的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 心 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1660s, back-formation from loquacity or else formed from stem of Latin loquax (genitive loquacis) "talkative," from loqui "to speak" (see locution) + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 四处搜寻， 细查； 擦洗， 擦亮； 冲刷出， 冲刷成
有说服力的；劝诱的，劝说的

etymology.story: 1580s, from Middle French persuasif, from Medieval Latin persuasivus, from Latin persuas-, past participle stem of persuadere "persuade, convince" (see persuasion).
late 15c., from Late Latin inculpabilis "unblamable," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + culpabilis (see culpable)..

meanings.definitionCn: 计算；数字的读法；[数] 命数法；编号

morphology.mnemonic: 数/数量 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin numerationem (nominative numeratio), noun of action from past participle stem of numerare "to count, number," from numerus "number" (see number (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 农村的，乡下的；田园的，有乡村风味的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French rural (14c.), from Latin ruralis "of the countryside," from rus (genitive ruris) "open land, country," from PIE *reue- (1) "to open; space" (see room (n.)).
mid-15c., from Middle French demise, fem.

late 14c., "causing relaxation or looseness," from Old French laxatif (13c.), from Medieval Latin laxativus "loosening," from Latin laxat-, past participle stem of laxare "loosen," from laxus "loose, lax" (see lax).

meanings.definitionCn: （疾病等）（全国、全球i）流行的；普遍的

etymology.story: 1660s, from Late Latin pandemus, from Greek pandemos "pertaining to all people; public, common," from pan- "all" (see pan-) + demos "people" (see demotic).

meanings.definitionCn: 疲倦；无精打采；柔情；呆滞
酸,酸性物质a.酸的,酸味的；尖刻的
传动装置，[机] 变速器；传递；传送；播送

etymology.story: 1610s, "conveyance from one place to another," from Latin transmissionem (nominative transmissio) "a sending over or across, passage," noun of action from past participle stem of transmittere "send over or across" (see transmit).

"sudden attack, convulsion," early 15c., from Middle French paroxysme (16c.), earlier paroxime (13c.), from Medieval Latin paroxysmus "irritation, fit of a disease," from Greek paroxysmos "irritation, exasperation," from paroxynein "to irritate, goad, provoke," from para- "beyond" (see para- (1)) + oxynein "sharpen, goad," from oxys "sharp, pointed" (see acrid).

mid-14c., from Old French negligence "negligence, sloth; injury, injustice" (12c.), and directly from Latin neclegentia, neglegentia "carelessness, heedlessness, neglect," from neglegentem (nominative neglegens) "heedless, careless, unconcerned," present participle of neglegere "to neglect" (see neglect (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 可疑的；暧昧的；无把握的；半信半疑的

etymology.story: 1540s, from Latin dubiosus "doubtful," from dubium "doubt," neuter of dubius "vacillating, moving two ways, fluctuating;" figuratively "wavering in opinion, doubting, doubtful," from duo "two" (see two), with a sense of "of two minds, undecided between two things." Old English also used tweo "two" to mean "doubt." Compare doubt (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 直觉；直觉力；直觉的知识

root.meaning: to perceive directly without reasoning, know by immediate perception

morphology.mnemonic: to perceive directly without reasoning, know by immediate perception + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., intuicioun, "insight, direct or immediate cognition, spiritual perception," originally theological, from Late Latin intuitionem (nominative intuitio) "a looking at, consideration," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin intueri "look at, consider," from in- "at, on" (see in- (2)) + tueri "to look at, watch over" (see tutor (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 快的；迅速的；敏捷的；立刻的
柔软的；易教导的；易延展的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French ductile or directly from Latin ductilis "that may be led or drawn," from past participle of ducere "to lead" (see duke (n.)).
late 14c., "to join or unite (something to something else)," from Latin addere "add to, join, attach, place upon," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + -dere comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 液体 + id + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1570s, "to reduce to order, to set out clearly" (of accounts), from Late Latin or Medieval Latin liquidatus, past participle of liquidare "to melt, make liquid or clear, clarify," from Latin liquidus (see liquid).
early 15c., from Latin accedere "approach, enter upon," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + cedere "go, move" (see cede).

mid-14c., from Old French ratifier (13c.), from Medieval Latin ratificare "confirm, approve," literally "fix by reckoning," from Latin ratus "fixed by calculation; determined; approved; certain, sure; valid" (past participle adjective from reri "to reckon, think;" see reason (v.)) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).
1660s (Milton), from Late Latin effulgentia (from Latin effulgentum; see effulgent)..

meanings.definitionCn: 使生动；使活跃；给与生气

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French vivifier "come alive; give life to" (12c.), from Late Latin vivificare "make alive, restore to life," from vivificus "enlivening," from Latin vivus "alive" (see vivid) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 节约的，节省的；有节制的

etymology.story: early 13c., "to free from faults, rectify," from Old French amender (12c.), from Latin emendare "to correct, free from fault," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + menda "fault, blemish," from PIE *mend- "physical defect, fault" (source also of Sanskrit minda "physical blemish," Old Irish mennar "stain, blemish," Welsh mann "sign, mark").

meanings.definitionCn: 有限的；[数]有穷的,限定的

etymology.story: early 15c., "limited in space or time, finite," from Latin finitum, past participle of finire "to limit, set bounds; come to an end" (see finish (v.)).
1869, so called because it consists of one carbon and one oxygen atom (as opposed to carbon dioxide, which has two of the latter).

meanings.definitionCn: 建设性的；推定的；构造上的；有助益的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 建造 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: early 15c., "derived by interpretation," from Middle French constructif or from Medieval Latin constructivus, from Latin construct-, past participle stem of construere "to heap up" (see construction).

mid-15c., from Anglo-French propicius, Old French propicius "gracious, favorable, useful" (12c., Modern French propice) and directly from Latin propitius "favorable, kind, gracious, well-disposed" (see propitiation).
1714, tanterum, originally colloquial, of unknown origin..
1590s, from Late Latin nullificare "to esteem lightly, despise," literally "to make nothing," from Latin nullus "not any" (see null) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

mid-15c., "wishing to do good, kindly," from Middle French benivolent and directly from Latin benevolentem (nominative benevolens) "wishing (someone) well, benevolent," related to benevolentia "good feeling" (see benevolence).

late 14c., "conflict, fighting; difficulty, trouble," compounded from at do, dialectal in Norse influenced areas of England for to do, as some Scandinavian languages used at with infinitive of a verb where Modern English uses to.

mid-14c., "remove, misappropriate," from Anglo-French purloigner "remove," Old French porloigner "put off, retard, delay, drag out; be far away," from por- (from Latin pro- "forth;" see pro-) + Old French loing "far," from Latin longe, from longus (see long (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 熔化， 熔合； 聚变

root.meaning: device that breaks an electrical circuit

morphology.mnemonic: device that breaks an electrical circuit + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1550s, "act of melting by heat," from Middle French fusion or directly from Latin fusionem (nominative fusio) "an outpouring, effusion," noun of action from fusus, past participle of fundere "to pour, melt" (see found (v.2)).
"flesh-eating animal," 1839, from French carnivore (16c.), from Latin carnivorus "flesh-eating" (see carnivorous)..

meanings.definitionCn: 药房；配药学，药剂学；制药业；一批备用药品

etymology.story: late 14c., "a medicine," from Old French farmacie "a purgative" (13c.), from Medieval Latin pharmacia, from Greek pharmakeia "use of drugs, medicines, potions, or spells; poisoning, witchcraft; remedy, cure," from pharmakeus (fem.

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + just + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: 1640s, from French ajustement or else a native formation from adjust (v.) + -ment..

morphology.mnemonic: rat + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1825, "a rendering rational," from rationalize + -ation.

meanings.definitionCn: [解剖] 生殖系统；繁殖方式

etymology.story: "treasonable or perfidious conduct," c.

morphology.mnemonic: 远离/脱离 + 起源/开始 + 电

etymology.story: 1858, mistaken singular of aborigines (1540s; the correct singular is aboriginal), from Latin Aborigines "the first ancestors of the Romans; the first inhabitants" (especially of Latium), possibly a tribal name, or from or made to conform to ab origine, literally "from the beginning." Extended 1789 to natives of other countries which Europeans have colonized.

meanings.definitionCn: 作伪证；使发伪誓；使破坏誓言

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 判断/法律

etymology.story: 1960, acronym for "light amplification by stimulated emission of radiation," on pattern of maser (1955).
early 15c., from Old French misture and directly from Latin mixtura "a mixing," from mixtus (see mix (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 范围；余地；视野；眼界；导弹射程

etymology.story: "to view," 1807, from the source of scope (n.2).
1660s, from French stratifier, from Modern Latin  stratificare, from stratum (see stratum).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治/首领 + 具有…特征/名词化
出/从 + 数/数量 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1640s, from or modeled on Latin enumeratus, past participle of enumerare (see enumeration).
1752, "moral principles or practice," from French morale "morality, good conduct," from fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 使移居国外；流放，放逐；使放弃国籍

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 父亲 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1768, modeled on French expatrier "banish" (14c.), from ex- "out of" (see ex-) + patrie "native land," from Latin patria "one's native country," from pater (genitive patris) "father" (see father (n.); also compare patriot).

meanings.definitionCn: 给…穿上裤子；给…装上炮尾

etymology.story: "back part of a gun or firearm," 1570s, from singular of breeches (q.v.)..
1530s, from French assailant, noun use of present participle of assailir (see assail).
late 14c., from Old French modifier (14c.), from Latin modificare "to limit, measure off, restrain," from modus "measure, manner" (see mode (n.1)) + root of facere "to make" (see factitious).

meanings.definitionCn: 坚定的, 不变的，不动摇的 "

etymology.story: Old English stedefæst "secure in position, steady, firm in its place," from stede  (see stead) + fæst (see fast (adj.)); similar formation in Middle Low German stedevast, Old Norse staðfastr "steadfast, firm; faithful, staunch, firm in one's mind." Of persons, in English, "unshakable, stubborn, resolute" from c.

root.meaning: device that breaks an electrical circuit

morphology.mnemonic: 出/从 + device that breaks an electrical circuit + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: "flowing profusely" (especially of words), 1660s, from Latin effus-, stem of effundere "to pour forth, spread abroad" (see effusion) + -ive.
mid-13c., from Anglo-French mariner, Old French marinier "seaman, sailor" (12c.), from Medieval Latin marinarius "sailor," from Latin marinus "of the sea" (see marine).

meanings.definitionCn: 罪恶的；恶棍的；恶毒的；坏透的

root.meaning: character in a novel, play, etc. whose evil motives or actions help drive the plot

morphology.mnemonic: character in a novel, play, etc. whose evil motives or actions help drive the plot + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., "to curse, to call God to witness and abhor," from Middle French détester, from Latin detestari "to curse, execrate, abominate, express abhorrence for," literally "denounce with one's testimony," from de- "from, down" (see de-) + testari "be a witness," from testis "witness" (see testament).
1630s, perhaps a formation in English from theory + -ize.
1680s, "a place where something tends to collect," originally figurative, from French réservoir "storehouse," from Old French reserver "to reserve" (see reserve (n.)).

Old English fægnian "rejoice, be glad, exult, applaud," from fægen "glad" (see fain); used in Middle English to refer to expressions of delight, especially a dog wagging its tail (early 13c.), hence "court favor, grovel, act slavishly" (early 14c.).
late 14c., a contraction of al be it "al(though) it be (that).".

meanings.definitionCn: 美的；美学的；审美的，具有审美趣味的

etymology.story: 1798, from German Ästhetisch or French esthétique, both from Greek aisthetikos "sensitive, perceptive," from aisthanesthai "to perceive (by the senses or by the mind), to feel," from PIE *awis-dh-yo-, from root *au- "to perceive" (see audience).

meanings.definitionCn: 不协调的， 不一致的； 不适宜的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + congru + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin incongruus "incongruous, inconsistent," from in- "not, opposite of, without" (see in- (1)) + congruus "fit, suitable" (see congruent).
1620s, from Medieval Latin tendentia "inclination, leaning," from Latin tendens, present participle of tendere "to stretch, extend, aim" (see tenet).

meanings.definitionCn: 报纸文章的标题；照片说明

etymology.story: mid-13c., arsmetike, from Old French arsmetique (12c.), from Latin arithmetica, from Greek arithmetike (tekhne) "(the) counting (art)," fem.
Old English cnihthad "the period between childhood and manhood;" see knight (n.) + -hood.

meanings.definitionCn: 变化；[生物] 变异，变种

etymology.story: late 14c., "difference, divergence," from Old French variacion "variety, diversity" and directly from Latin variationem (nominative variatio) "a difference, variation, change," from past participle stem of variare "to change" (see vary).

meanings.definitionCn: 潜在意识；下意识心理活动

etymology.story: 1823, "not wholly conscious, feebly conscious" (implied in subconsciously), from sub- + conscious.
early 15c., from Middle French codicille, from Latin codicillus "a short writing, a small writing tablet," diminutive of codex (genitive codicis), see code (n.)..
mid-15c., from Latin eiectus "thrown out," past participle of eicere "throw out, cast out, thrust out; drive into exile, expel, drive away," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + -icere, comb.

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 做/制造

etymology.story: 1570s, from Latin defectus, past participle of deficere "to fail, desert" (see defect (n.)).

in wagering sense, found first in Shakespeare ("2 Henry IV," 1597), probably from earlier sense of "amount by which one thing exceeds or falls short of another" (1540s), from odd (q.v.), though the sense evolution is uncertain.
"highest point," 1560s, from Greek akme "(highest) point, edge; peak of anything," from PIE root *ak- "sharp" (see acrid).

meanings.definitionCn: 随便的；非正式的；临时的；偶然的
便装；临时工人；待命士兵

etymology.story: late 14c., "subject to or produced by chance," from Middle French casuel (15c.), from Late Latin casualis "by chance," from Latin casus "chance, occasion, opportunity; accident, event" (see case (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 费解的；不可思议的；无限的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + 共同/联合 + 抓住/理解 + able

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French incomprehensible or directly from Latin incomprehensibilis "that cannot be seized or appreciated," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + comprehensibilis (see comprehensible).

meanings.definitionCn: 沼泽；困境；乱糟糟的一堆

etymology.story: "wet, swampy tract," 1650s, from Dutch moeras "marsh, fen," from Middle Dutch marasch, from Old French marais "marsh," from Frankish, possibly from West Germanic *marisk, from Proto-Germanic *mariskaz "like a lake," from *mari "sea" (see mere (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 远 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1882, coined (along with telæsthesia) by English psychologist Frederic Myers (1843-1901), literally "feeling from afar," from tele- + -pathy.

late 14c., frenetik, "temporarily deranged, delirious, crazed," from Old French frenetike "mad, crazy" (13c.), from Latin phreneticus "delirious," alteration of Greek phrenitikos, from phrenitis "frenzy," literally "inflammation of the brain," from phren "mind, reason," also "diaphragm" (see phreno-) + -itis "inflammation." The classical ph- sometimes was restored from mid-16c.

1734, from French nonchalant, present participle of nonchaloir "be indifferent to, have no concern for" (13c.), from non- "not" (see non-) + chaloir "have concern for," ultimately from Latin calere "be hot" (see calorie).
1748, "place where shrubs are planted," from shrub + -ery.

meanings.definitionCn: 剧本(原稿)；手稿，原稿；笔迹，手迹

etymology.story: late 13c., "style or fashion of attire," from Old French guise "manner, fashion, way," from Frankish *wisa or some similar Germanic source, from Proto-Germanic *wison "appearance, form, manner," from *wissaz (source also of Old High German wisa "manner, wise"), from PIE root *weid- "to see" (see vision).

morphology.mnemonic: like + 以…方式/副词化 + hood

etymology.story: late 14c., "resemblance, similarity," from likely + -hood.

meanings.definitionCn: 保守的，反动的；反动主义的；反对改革的
反动分子；反动派；保守派

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 做/驱动 + 状态/行为/过程 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: 1831, on model of French réactionnaire (19c.), from réaction (see reaction).

meanings.definitionCn: 轻率的，鲁莽的；不小心的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + prud + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin imprudentem (nominative imprudens) "not foreseeing, unaware, inconsiderate, heedless," from assimilated form of in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + prudens, contraction of providens, present participle of providere "to provide," literally "to see before (one)" (see provide).

1690s, originally medical, "forceful, vigorous, especially in effect on bowels," from Greek drastikos "effective, efficacious; active, violent," from drasteon "(thing) to be done," from dran "to do, act, perform." Sense of "extreme, severe" is first recorded 1808.

meanings.definitionCn: 可原谅的；（罪过）轻微的；可宽恕的

etymology.story: 1540s, "introduce as new" (trans.), from Latin innovatus, past participle of innovare "to renew, restore;" also "to change," from in- "into" (see in- (2)) + novus "new" (see new).

morphology.mnemonic: vapor + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: also vaporisation, 1788, noun of action from vaporize.

meanings.definitionCn: 韧性；固执；不屈不挠；黏性

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French ténacité (14c.) and directly from Latin tenacitas "an act of holding fast," from tenax (genitive tenacis) "holding fast, gripping, clingy; firm, steadfast," from tenere "to hold" (see tenet)..

meanings.definitionCn: 主角，主演；主要人物，领导者

etymology.story: 1670s, "principal character in a story, drama, etc.," from Greek protagonistes "actor who plays the chief or first part," from protos "first" (see proto-) + agonistes "actor, competitor," from agon "contest" (see act (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 断言，声明；主张，要求；坚持；认定

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., assercioun, from Middle French assertion (14c.) or directly from Late Latin assertionem (nominative assertio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin asserere "claim rights over something, state, maintain, affirm," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + serere "join" (see series).

meanings.definitionCn: 搪塞；支吾其辞，闪烁其辞

etymology.story: 1580s, "to transgress," a back formation from prevarication, or else from Latin praevaricatus, past participle of praevaricari "to make a sham accusation, deviate," literally "walk crookedly;" in Church Latin, "to transgress" (see prevarication).
1788, "that which results from something," originally Scottish, from out + come (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 极地的；两极的；正好相反的

morphology.mnemonic: to push with a pole + 与…有关的/像…的

etymology.story: 1550s, from Middle French polaire (16c.) or directly from Medieval Latin polaris "of or pertaining to the poles," from Latin polus "an end of an axis" (see pole (n.2)).

1610s, "deductive reasoning," from Latin synthesis "collection, set, suit of clothes, composition (of a medication)," from Greek synthesis "composition, a putting together," from syntithenai "put together, combine," from syn- "together" (see syn-) + tithenai "put, place" (see theme).

meanings.definitionCn: 广告客户；刊登广告的人

etymology.story: 1560s, agent noun from advertise (v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 神秘的，含义模糊的；[动] 隐藏的

etymology.story: 1630s, "hidden, occult, mystical," from Late Latin crypticus, from Greek kryptikos "fit for concealing," from kryptos "hidden" (see crypt).

early 15c., from Latin circumspectus "deliberate, guarded, well-considered," past participle of circumspicere "look around, take heed," from circum- "around" (see circum-) + specere "to look" (see scope (n.1)).

1610s, "a general view, an outline," from Late Latin synopsis "a synopsis," from Greek synopsis "a general view," literally "a seeing altogether, a seeing all at once," from syn- "together" (see syn-) + opsis "sight, appearance," from PIE *okw- "to see" (see eye (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 想,考虑,(尤指宗教上的)沉思,冥想

etymology.story: 1580s, "to ponder," back-formation from meditation, or else from Latin meditatus, past participle of meditari (see meditation).

meanings.definitionCn: 人口统计学的； 人口的

etymology.story: 1891, from demography + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 回忆,想起,记起,忆起,记得

etymology.story: "remember, recover knowledge of," 1550s, from Latin recollectus, past participle of recolligere, literally "to collect again," from re- "again" (see re-) + colligere "gather" (see collect).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French altercacion (12c.) and directly from Latin altercationem (nominative altercatio) "a dispute, debate, discussion," noun of action from past participle stem of altercari "to dispute (with another)," from alter "other" (see alter)..

mid-15c., "to summon," from Old French citer "to summon" (14c.), from Latin citare "to summon, urge, call; put in sudden motion, call forward; rouse, excite," frequentative of ciere "to move, set in motion, stir, rouse, call, invite" from PIE root *keie- "to set in motion, to move to and fro" (source also of Sanskrit cyavate "stirs himself, goes;" Greek kinein "to move, set in motion; change, stir up," kinymai "move myself;" Gothic haitan "call, be called;" Old English hatan "command, call").

late 15c., as a hypothetical indivisible body, the building block of the universe, from Latin atomus (especially in Lucretius) "indivisible particle," from Greek atomos "uncut, unhewn; indivisible," from a- "not" + tomos "a cutting," from temnein "to cut" (see tome).

meanings.definitionCn: 想起，忆起；使……思考；使……考虑

etymology.story: reflexive verb, Old English beþencan "to consider," from be- + þencan "to think" (see think).

meanings.definitionCn: 声名狼藉的；无耻的；邪恶的；不名誉的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + fa + 具有…性质的

meanings.definitionCn: 平凡的，乏味的；散文体的

etymology.story: 1650s, "having to do with prose," from Middle French prosaique and directly from Medieval Latin prosaicus "in prose" (16c.), from Latin prosa "prose" (see prose).

meanings.definitionCn: 无经验的人；刚会飞的幼鸟

etymology.story: also fledgeling, 1830, "untried" (adj.), in Tennyson; 1846 as a noun meaning "young bird" (one newly fledged); from fledge + diminutive suffix -ling.

1630s, "a cutting off (mentally), abstraction," from French précision (16c.) and directly from Latin praecisionem (nominative praecisio) "a cutting off," noun of action from past participle stem of praecidere (see precise).

meanings.definitionCn: 总督；（北美）一种黑色蝴蝶

etymology.story: person ruling as representative of a sovereign, 1520s, from Middle French vice-roy, from Old French vice- "deputy" (see vice-) + roi "king," from Latin regem (nominative rex); see rex.

meanings.definitionCn: 肯定的；独断的；坚定而自信的

etymology.story: 1560s, "declaratory, positive, full of assertion," from assert + -ive.

meanings.definitionCn: 可忍受的，可容许的，尚好的

etymology.story: early 15c., "bearable," from Middle French tolerable (14c.) and directly from Latin tolerabilis "that may be endured, supportable, passable," from tolerare "to tolerate" (see toleration).

meanings.definitionCn: 身体；主体；大量；团体；主要部分

etymology.story: Old English bodig "trunk, chest" (of a man or animal); related to Old High German botah, of unknown origin.
1640s, from Middle French elapser, from Latin elapsus, past participle of elabi "slip or glide away, escape," from ex- "out, away" (see ex-) + labi "to slip, glide" (see lapse (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: sy + 测量 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1560s, "relation of parts, proportion," from Middle French symmétrie (16c.) and directly from Latin symmetria, from Greek symmetria "agreement in dimensions, due proportion, arrangement," from symmetros "having a common measure, even, proportionate," from assimilated form of syn- "together" (see syn-) + metron "meter" (see meter (n.2)).
1540s, "quality of being acrid," from Middle French acrimonie or directly from Latin acrimonia "sharpness, pungency of taste," figuratively "acrimony, severity, energy," from acer "sharp" (fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 报应；给与惩罚的人；天罚

etymology.story: 1570s, Nemesis, "Greek goddess of vengeance, personification of divine wrath," from Greek nemesis "just indignation, righteous anger," literally "distribution" (of what is due), related to nemein "distribute, allot, apportion one's due," from PIE root *nem- "to divide, distribute, allot, to take" (source also of Old English, Gothic niman "to take," German nehmen; see nimble).
1640s, from French retoucher (13c.) "to touch again" (with a view to improving), from re- "again" (see re-) + toucher (see touch (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: [计] 缓冲区；缓冲器，[车辆] 减震器

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 填充/补充

meanings.definitionCn: 限制；使具有资格；证明…合格

etymology.story: mid-15c., "to invest with a quality," from Middle French qualifier (15c.) and directly from Medieval Latin qualificare "attribute a quality to; make of a certain quality," from Latin qualis "of what sort?," correlative pronominal adjective (see quality) + facere "to make" (see factitious).
1650s; see abhorrent + -ence..
1590s, from Middle French adhérer (15c.) or directly from Latin adhaerare "to stick to" (see adherent (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 无趣味的；无生气的；索然乏味的

etymology.story: 1650s, "flat, insipid" (of drinks), from Latin vapidus "flat, insipid," literally "that has exhaled its vapor," related to vappa "stale wine," and probably to vapor "vapor." Applied from 1758 to talk and writing deemed dull and lifeless.

1540s, "matter which settles at the bottom of water or other liquid," from Middle French sédiment (16c.) and directly from Latin sedimentum "a settling, sinking down," from stem of sedere "to settle, sit" (see sedentary)..
1610s, from Latin tremulus "shaking, quivering," from tremere "to shake, quake, quiver" (see tremble (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 单位，单元；装置；[军] 部队；部件

suffix.meaning: thing or animal spoken about before.

morphology.mnemonic: un + thing or animal spoken about before.

etymology.story: 1560s, "single number regarded as an undivided whole," alteration of unity on the basis of digit.

meanings.definitionCn: 全景,全景画,全景摄影,全景照片[装置]

etymology.story: 1796, "a painting on a revolving cylindrical surface," coined c.

meanings.definitionCn: 现代的,当代的;同时代的 time

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 时间 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: "one who lives at the same time as another," 1630s, originally cotemporary, from co- + temporary; modified by influence of contemporary (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 有实质的；大量的；真实的；独立存在的
名词性实词；独立存在的实体

morphology.mnemonic: any kind of corporeal matter + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: in grammatical use, late 14c., short for noun substantive, from Late Latin substantivium, neuter of substantivus "of substance or being" (see substantive (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 愁容；怒容；阴沉沉的样子
仿制的，模拟的，虚假的，不诚实的

morphology.mnemonic: 土地 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1620s, "of or pertaining to a territory," from Late Latin territorialis, from territorium (see territory).

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1947, from an adjective (1865), a shortened form of sensory (q.v.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 信任；凭证；祭器台（等于credence table，credenza）

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Medieval Latin credentia "belief," from Latin credentum (nominative credens), past participle of credere "believe, trust" (see credo)..
1610s, "make easy, render less difficult," from French faciliter "to render easy," from stem of Latin facilis "easy" (see facile).

late 12c., from Old French rober "rob, steal, pillage, ransack, rape," from West Germanic *rauba "booty" (source also of Old High German roubon "to rob," roub "spoil, plunder;" Old English reafian, source of the reave in bereave), from Proto-Germanic *raubon "to rob," from PIE *reup-, *reub- "to snatch" (see rip (v.)).To rob the cradle is attested from 1864 in reference to drafting young men in the American Civil War; by 1949 in reference to seductions or romantic relationships with younger persons.

meanings.definitionCn: 青翠的；翠绿的；没有经验的；不老练的

etymology.story: 1580s, "green in color; green with vegetation," from Middle French virdeant "becoming green," present participle of Old French verdeiier "become green," from Vulgar Latin *viridiare "grow green, make green," from Latin viridis "green" (see verdure).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "act of coming to a position," especially of a throne, 1640s, from Latin accessionem (nominative accessio) "a going to, joining, increase," noun of action from past participle stem of accedere "approach, enter upon" (see accede)..

meanings.definitionCn: 浮力；轻快；轻松的心情；（股票）保持高价或回升

morphology.mnemonic: rise up, lift, sustain + ance + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1713, from buoyant + -cy.

meanings.definitionCn: 反抗的；造反的；难控制的

root.meaning: supporter of the American cause in the War of Independence

morphology.mnemonic: supporter of the American cause in the War of Independence + 具有…性质的

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin rebellis (see rebel (adj.)) + -ous.

meanings.definitionCn: 散布谣言；煽动行为；群众煽动

etymology.story: 1855; see demagogue + -ery.

1620s (implied in predicted), "foretell, prophesy," a back formation from prediction or else from Latin praedicatus, past participle of praedicere "foretell, advise, give notice," from prae "before" (see pre-) + dicere "to say" (see diction).
also linch-pin, late 14c., earlier linspin, from Middle English lins "axletree" (see linch) + pin (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 破坏，渐渐破坏；挖掘地基
日落；天变黑（gloam的现在分词）

etymology.story: Old English glomung "twilight, the fall of evening," found but once (glossing Latin crepusculum), and formed (probably on model of æfning "evening") from glom "twilight," which is related to glowan "to glow" (hence "glow of sunrise or sunset"), from Proto-Germanic *glo- (see glow (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之下 + 投/掷 + 有…倾向的

meanings.definitionCn: 基本的， 重要的， 根本的；激进的， 极端的

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 钝的，不锋利的；生硬的；直率的

etymology.story: street slang for "marijuana and tobacco cigar" (easier to pass around, easier to disguise, and the stimulant in the tobacco enhances the high from the pot) surfaced c.

meanings.definitionCn: 因情况而异的；不一定的；偶然发生的；可能的；依情况而定的；偶然的；[逻]有条件的
分遣队；偶然事件；分得部分；代表团

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French contingent or directly from Latin contingentem (nominative contingens) "happening, touching," present participle of contingere "to touch" (see contact).

morphology.mnemonic: 相同 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1510s, "gray or white with age" (of hair); c.
early 15c., "chopped small," from Latin minutus "little, small, minute," past participle of minuere "to lessen, diminish" (see minus).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1540s (perhaps early 15c.), from Old French analogie or directly from Latin analogia, from Greek analogia "proportion," from ana- "upon, according to" (see ana-) + logos "ratio," also "word, speech, reckoning" (see logos).

1570s, from Middle French acerbité, from Latin acerbitatem (nominative acerbitas) "harshness, sharpness, bitterness," from acerbus "bitter to taste, sharp, sour, tart" (related to acer "sharp;" compare Latin superbus "haughty," from super "above"), from Proto-Italic *akro-po- "sharp," from PIE *ak- "sharp" (see acrid).

meanings.definitionCn: 卓越的；超常的；出类拔萃的

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Latin transcendentem (nominative transcendens) "surmounting, rising above," present participle of transcendere (see transcend).

meanings.definitionCn: 芬芳的；有…香味的；令人想起…的

etymology.story: 1746, from mile + stone (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 生产(性)的,能产的,多产的

morphology.mnemonic: 向前/支持 + 引导 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: 1610s, from French productif (16c.) and directly from Medieval Latin productivus "fit for production," from Latin product-, past participle stem of producere (see produce (v.)).
1650s, from out + burst (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: 低于/不足 + thes + 过程/状态

etymology.story: 1590s, "a particular statement;" 1650s, "a proposition, assumed and taken for granted, used as a premise," from Middle French hypothese and directly from Late Latin hypothesis, from Greek hypothesis "base, groundwork, foundation," hence in extended use "basis of an argument, supposition," literally "a placing under," from hypo- "under" (see hypo-) + thesis "a placing, proposition" (see thesis).

meanings.definitionCn: 极其丰富的；很多的；大量的

etymology.story: early 15c., "lavish, extravagant," from Latin profusus "spread out, lavish, extravagant," literally "poured forth," noun use of past participle of profundere "pour forth," from pro- "forth" (see pro-) + fundere "to pour" (see found (v.2)).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 抓住/理解
